ipc_sections_reference.description: Complete reference index of all IPC sections under the Indian Penal Code, 1860 (including sub-sections and amendments as applicable). Note: The IPC was replaced by the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) 2023 with effect from July 1, 2024, but remains applicable to cases filed before that date.

sections.title: Title and Extent of Operation of the Code
sections.description: This Act shall be called the Indian Penal Code and shall extend to the whole of India.

sections.title: Punishment of Offences Committed Within India
sections.description: Every person shall be liable to punishment under this Code for every act or omission contrary to the provisions thereof within India.

sections.title: Punishment of Offences Committed Beyond India
sections.description: Any person liable by any law to be tried for an offence committed beyond India shall be dealt with according to this Code.

sections.title: Extension of Code to Extra-Territorial Offences
sections.description: The provisions of this Code apply to any offence committed by a citizen of India in any place without India.

sections.title: Certain Laws Not to Be Affected by This Act
sections.description: Nothing in this Act shall affect the provisions of any Act for punishing mutiny and desertion of officers, soldiers, sailors or airmen.

sections.title: Definitions Subject to Exceptions
sections.description: Every definition, penal provision, and illustration shall be understood subject to the exceptions in Chapter IV.

sections.title: Sense of Expression Once Explained
sections.description: Every expression which is explained in any part of this Code is used in every part of this Code in conformity with the explanation.

sections.title: Gender
sections.description: The pronoun 'he' and its derivatives are used of any person whether male or female.

sections.title: Number
sections.description: Unless the contrary appears from the context, words importing the singular number include the plural, and vice versa.

sections.title: Man and Woman
sections.description: 'Man' denotes a male human being of any age; 'woman' denotes a female human being of any age.

sections.title: Person
sections.description: The word 'person' includes any company or association or body of persons, whether incorporated or not.

sections.title: Public
sections.description: The word 'public' includes any class of the public or any community.

sections.title: Queen (Government)
sections.description: Refers to the Government of India in the modern context.

sections.title: Servant of Government
sections.description: Denotes any officer or servant continued, appointed or employed in India by or under the authority of the Government.

sections.title: British India
sections.description: Historical definition; now refers to the territory of India.

sections.title: Government of India
sections.description: Refers to the Central Government of India.

sections.title: Government
sections.description: The word 'Government' denotes the Central Government or the Government of a State.

sections.title: India
sections.description: 'India' means the territory of India.

sections.title: Judge
sections.description: Denotes a person who is empowered by law to give a definitive judgment in a civil, criminal, ecclesiastical or military proceeding.

sections.title: Court of Justice
sections.description: Denotes a Judge who is empowered by law to act judicially alone, or a body of Judges empowered by law to act judicially as a body.

sections.title: Public Servant
sections.description: Every person in the service or pay of the Government including military, judicial, election and other officers falling under enumerated categories.

sections.title: Movable Property
sections.description: Intended to include corporeal property of every description, except land and things attached to the earth or permanently fastened to anything attached to the earth.

sections.title: Wrongful Gain and Wrongful Loss
sections.description: 'Wrongful gain' is gain by unlawful means of property to which the person gaining is not legally entitled. 'Wrongful loss' is loss by unlawful means of property to which the person losing it is legally entitled.

sections.title: Dishonestly
sections.description: Whoever does anything with the intention of causing wrongful gain to one person or wrongful loss to another, is said to do that thing 'dishonestly'.

sections.title: Fraudulently
sections.description: A person is said to do a thing 'fraudulently' if he does that thing with intent to defraud but not otherwise.

sections.title: Reason to Believe
sections.description: A person is said to have 'reason to believe' a thing if he has sufficient cause to believe that thing but not otherwise.

sections.title: Property in Possession of Wife, Clerk or Servant
sections.description: When property is in the possession of a person's wife, clerk or servant on account of that person, it is in that person's possession within the meaning of this Code.

sections.title: Counterfeit
sections.description: A person is said to 'counterfeit' who causes one thing to resemble another thing intending by means of that resemblance to practise deception, or knowing it to be likely that deception will thereby be practised.

sections.title: Document
sections.description: Denotes any matter expressed or described upon any substance by means of letters, figures, or marks intended to be used as evidence of that matter.

sections.title: Electronic Record
sections.description: Shall have the meaning assigned in the Information Technology Act, 2000.

sections.title: Valuable Security
sections.description: Denotes a document which is, or purports to be, a document whereby any legal right is created, extended, transferred, restricted, extinguished or released.

sections.title: A Will
sections.description: Denotes any testamentary document.

sections.title: Words Referring to Acts Include Illegal Omissions
sections.description: In every part of this Code, except where a contrary intention appears from the context, words which refer to acts done extend also to illegal omissions.

sections.title: Act and Omission
sections.description: The word 'act' denotes as well a series of acts as a single act; the word 'omission' denotes as well a series of omissions as a single omission.

sections.title: Acts Done by Several Persons in Furtherance of Common Intention
sections.description: When a criminal act is done by several persons in furtherance of the common intention of all, each of such persons is liable for that act in the same manner as if it were done by him alone.

sections.title: When Such an Act Is Criminal by Reason of Being Done with Criminal Knowledge or Intention
sections.description: Whenever an act, which is criminal only by reason of its being done with a criminal knowledge or intention, is done by several persons, each of such persons who joins in the act with such knowledge or intention is liable for the act in the same manner as if the act were done by him alone with that knowledge or intention.

sections.title: Effect Caused Partly by Act and Partly by Omission
sections.description: Wherever the causing of a certain effect, or an attempt to cause that effect, by an act or by an omission, is an offence, it is to be understood that the causing of that effect partly by an act and partly by an omission is the same offence.

sections.title: Co-operation by Doing One of Several Acts Constituting an Offence
sections.description: When an offence is committed by means of several acts, whoever intentionally co-operates in the commission of that offence by doing any one of those acts, whether alone or jointly with any other person, commits that offence.

sections.title: Persons Concerned in Criminal Act May Be Guilty of Different Offences
sections.description: Where several persons are engaged or concerned in the commission of a criminal act, they may be guilty of different offences by means of that act.

sections.title: Voluntarily
sections.description: A person is said to cause an effect 'voluntarily' when he causes it by means whereby he intended to cause it, or by means which, at the time of employing those means, he knew or had reason to believe to be likely to cause it.

sections.title: Offence
sections.description: Denotes a thing made punishable by this Code. Also includes things punishable under other laws when referred to in Chapters IV and V.

sections.title: Special Law
sections.description: A law applicable to a particular subject.

sections.title: Local Law
sections.description: A law applicable only within certain local limits.

sections.title: Illegal and Legally Bound to Do
sections.description: 'Illegal' applies to everything which is an offence, or which is prohibited by law, or which furnishes ground for a civil action. A person is 'legally bound to do' whatever it is illegal in him to omit.

sections.title: Injury
sections.description: The word 'injury' denotes any harm whatever illegally caused to any person, in body, mind, reputation or property.

sections.title: Life
sections.description: The word 'life' denotes the life of a human being, unless the contrary appears from the context.

sections.title: Death
sections.description: The word 'death' denotes the death of a human being unless the contrary appears from the context.

sections.title: Animal
sections.description: The word 'animal' denotes any living creature, other than a human being.

sections.title: Vessel
sections.description: The word 'vessel' denotes anything made for the conveyance by water of human beings or of property.

sections.title: Year and Month
sections.description: Wherever the word 'year' or 'month' is used, it is to be understood that the year or the month is to be reckoned according to the British calendar.

sections.title: Section
sections.description: The word 'section' denotes one of those portions of a Chapter of this Code which are distinguished by prefixed numeral figures.

sections.title: Oath
sections.description: The word 'oath' includes a solemn affirmation substituted by law for an oath, and any declaration required or authorised by law to be made before a public servant or to be used for the purpose of proof, whether in a judicial proceeding or otherwise.

sections.title: Good Faith
sections.description: Nothing is said to be done or believed in 'good faith' which is done or believed without due care and attention.

sections.title: Harbour
sections.description: Except in sections 130 and 216A, 'harbour' includes the supplying a person with shelter, food, drink, money, clothes, arms, ammunition or means of conveyance, or the assisting a person in any way to evade apprehension.

sections.title: Punishments
sections.description: The punishments to which offenders are liable under this Code are: death; imprisonment for life; imprisonment (rigorous or simple); forfeiture of property; and fine.

sections.title: Construction of Reference to Transportation
sections.description: Reference to transportation for life shall be construed as a reference to imprisonment for life.

sections.title: Commutation of Sentence of Death
sections.description: The appropriate Government may, without the consent of the offender, commute any punishment awarded under this Code to any other punishment provided by this Code.

sections.title: Commutation of Sentence of Imprisonment for Life
sections.description: The appropriate Government may commute a sentence of imprisonment for life for imprisonment of either description for a term not exceeding fourteen years.

sections.title: Definition of Appropriate Government
sections.description: Defines 'appropriate Government' for commutation purposes.

sections.title: Sentence of Europeans and Americans to Penal Servitude
sections.description: Historical provision; now obsolete.

sections.title: Fractions of Terms of Punishment
sections.description: In calculating fractions of terms of punishment, imprisonment for life shall be reckoned as equivalent to imprisonment for twenty years.

sections.title: Offenders Sentenced to Transportation How Dealt With Until Transported
sections.description: Historical provision regarding transportation sentences.

sections.title: Transportation Instead of Imprisonment
sections.description: Historical provision; now obsolete.

sections.title: Sentence May Be Wholly or Partly Rigorous or Simple
sections.description: In every case in which an offender is punishable with imprisonment which may be of either description, it shall be competent to the Court which sentences such offender to direct in the sentence that such imprisonment shall be wholly rigorous, or wholly simple, or that any part of such imprisonment shall be rigorous and the rest simple.

sections.title: Sentence of Forfeiture of Property
sections.description: Historical provision.

sections.title: Forfeiture of Property in Respect of Offenders Punishable with Death or Transportation
sections.description: Historical provision.

sections.title: Amount of Fine
sections.description: Where no sum is expressed to which a fine may extend, the amount of fine to which the offender is liable is unlimited, but shall not be excessive.

sections.title: Sentence of Imprisonment for Non-Payment of Fine
sections.description: In every case of an offence punishable with imprisonment as well as fine in which the offender is sentenced to fine, it shall be competent to the court to direct that in default of payment the offender shall suffer imprisonment for a certain term.

sections.title: Limit to Imprisonment for Non-Payment of Fine When Imprisonment and Fine Awardable
sections.description: The term for which the court directs the offender to be imprisoned in default of payment of a fine shall not exceed one-fourth of the term of imprisonment which is the maximum fixed for the offence.

sections.title: Description of Imprisonment for Non-Payment of Fine
sections.description: The imprisonment which the court imposes in default of payment of a fine may be of any description to which the offender might have been sentenced for the offence.

sections.title: Imprisonment for Non-Payment of Fine When Offence Punishable with Fine Only
sections.description: If the offence be punishable with fine only, the imprisonment which the court imposes in default of payment shall be simple and the term shall not exceed specified limits.

sections.title: Imprisonment to Terminate on Payment of Fine
sections.description: The imprisonment which is imposed in default of payment of a fine shall terminate whenever that fine is either paid or levied by process of law.

sections.title: Termination of Imprisonment on Payment of Proportional Part of Fine
sections.description: If, before the expiration of the term of imprisonment fixed in default of payment, such a proportion of the fine be paid or levied that the term of imprisonment suffered in default of payment is not less than proportional to the part of the fine still unpaid, the imprisonment shall terminate.

sections.title: Fine Leviable Within Six Years, or During Imprisonment
sections.description: The fine, or any part thereof which remains unpaid, may be levied at any time within six years after the passing of the sentence, and if, under the sentence, the offender is liable to imprisonment for a longer period than six years, then at any time previous to the expiration of that period.

sections.title: Limit of Punishment of Offence Made Up of Several Offences
sections.description: Where anything which is an offence is made up of parts, any of which parts is itself an offence, the offender shall not be punished with the punishment of more than one of such his offences, unless it be so expressly provided.

sections.title: Punishment of Person Guilty of One of Several Offences, the Judgment Stating That It Is Doubtful of Which
sections.description: In all cases in which judgment is given that a person is guilty of one of several offences specified in the judgment, but that it is doubtful of which of these offences he is guilty, the offender shall be punished for the offence for which the lowest punishment is provided.

sections.title: Solitary Confinement
sections.description: Whenever any person is convicted of an offence for which the Court has power to sentence him to rigorous imprisonment, the Court may direct that the offender shall be kept in solitary confinement for any portion of the imprisonment.

sections.title: Limit of Solitary Confinement
sections.description: In executing a sentence of solitary confinement, such confinement shall in no case exceed fourteen days at a time, with intervals between the periods of solitary confinement of not less than such periods.

sections.title: Enhanced Punishment for Certain Offences After Previous Conviction
sections.description: Whoever, having been convicted of an offence punishable under Chapter XII or XVII with three years' imprisonment or upwards, is afterwards guilty of any offence punishable under either of those Chapters with imprisonment, shall be subject to enhanced punishment.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years

sections.title: Act Done by a Person Bound by Law
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who is, or who by reason of a mistake of fact believes himself to be, bound by law to do it.

sections.title: Act of Judge When Acting Judicially
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a Judge when acting judicially in the exercise of any power which is, or which in good faith he believes to be, given to him by law.

sections.title: Act Done Pursuant to the Judgment or Order of Court
sections.description: Nothing which is done in pursuance of, or which is warranted by the judgment or order of, a Court of Justice is an offence, notwithstanding the Court may have had no jurisdiction to pass such judgment or order.

sections.title: Act Done by a Person Justified by Law
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by any person who is justified by law, or who by reason of a mistake of fact in good faith believes himself to be justified by law.

sections.title: Accident in Doing a Lawful Act
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by accident or misfortune, and without any criminal intention or knowledge in the doing of a lawful act in a lawful manner by lawful means and with proper care and caution.

sections.title: Act Likely to Cause Harm Done Without Criminal Intent to Prevent Other Harm
sections.description: Nothing is an offence merely by reason of its being done with the knowledge that it is likely to cause harm, if it be done without any criminal intention to cause harm, and in good faith for the purpose of preventing or avoiding other harm.

sections.title: Act of a Child Under Seven Years of Age
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a child under seven years of age.

sections.title: Act of a Child Above Seven and Under Twelve of Immature Understanding
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a child above seven years of age and under twelve, who has not attained sufficient maturity of understanding to judge of the nature and consequences of his conduct on that occasion.

sections.title: Act of a Person of Unsound Mind
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who, at the time of doing it, by reason of unsoundness of mind, is incapable of knowing the nature of the act, or that he is doing what is either wrong or contrary to law.

sections.title: Act of a Person Incapable by Reason of Intoxication Caused Against His Will
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done by a person who, at the time of doing it, is, by reason of intoxication, incapable of knowing the nature of the act, provided the intoxicant was administered to him without his knowledge or against his will.

sections.title: Offence Requiring Particular Intent Committed by One Who Is Intoxicated
sections.description: In cases where an act done is not an offence unless done with a particular knowledge or intent, a person who does the act in a state of intoxication shall be liable to be dealt with as if he had the same knowledge as he would have had if he had not been intoxicated, unless the thing which intoxicated him was administered to him without his knowledge or against his will.

sections.title: Act Not Intended or Known to Be Likely to Cause Death Done by Consent
sections.description: Nothing which is not intended to cause death, or grievous hurt, and which is not known by the doer to be likely to cause death or grievous hurt, is an offence by reason of any harm which it may cause, to any person above eighteen years of age who has given consent.

sections.title: Act Not Intended to Cause Death Done by Consent in Good Faith for Person's Benefit
sections.description: Nothing which is not intended to cause death is an offence by reason of any harm which it may cause, or be intended by the doer to cause, or be known by the doer to be likely to cause, to any person for whose benefit it is done in good faith, and who has given a consent.

sections.title: Act Done in Good Faith for Benefit of Child or Insane Person by Guardian
sections.description: Nothing which is done in good faith for the benefit of a person under twelve years of age, or of unsound mind, by or by consent, either express or implied, of the guardian or other person having lawful charge of that person, is an offence by reason of any harm which it may cause, or be intended by the doer to cause or be known by the doer to be likely to cause to that person.

sections.title: Consent Known to Be Given Under Fear or Misconception
sections.description: A consent is not such a consent as is intended by any section of this Code, if the consent is given by a person under fear of injury, or under a misconception of fact, and if the person doing the act knows, or has reason to believe, that the consent was given in consequence of such fear or misconception.

sections.title: Exclusion of Acts Which Are Offences Independently of Harm Caused
sections.description: The exceptions in sections 87, 88 and 89 do not extend to acts which are offences independently of any harm which they may cause, or be intended to cause, or be known to be likely to cause, to the person giving the consent.

sections.title: Act Done in Good Faith for Benefit of a Person Without Consent
sections.description: Nothing is an offence by reason of any harm which it may cause to a person for whose benefit it is done in good faith, even without that person's consent, if the circumstances of the case are such that it is impossible for that person to signify consent.

sections.title: Communication Made in Good Faith
sections.description: No communication made in good faith is an offence by reason of any harm to the person to whom it is made, if it is made for the benefit of that person.

sections.title: Act to Which a Person Is Compelled by Threats
sections.description: Except murder, and offences against the State punishable with death, nothing is an offence which is done by a person who is compelled to do it by threats which reasonably cause the apprehension that instant death will otherwise be the consequence.

sections.title: Act Causing Slight Harm
sections.description: Nothing is an offence by reason that it causes, or that it is intended to cause, or that it is known to be likely to cause, any harm, if that harm is so slight that no person of ordinary sense and temper would complain of such harm.

sections.title: Things Done in Private Defence
sections.description: Nothing is an offence which is done in the exercise of the right of private defence.

sections.title: Right of Private Defence of the Body and Property
sections.description: Every person has a right to defend his own body, and the body of any other person, against any offence affecting the human body; and the property, whether movable or immovable, of himself or of any other person, against theft, robbery, mischief or criminal trespass.

sections.title: Right of Private Defence Against the Act of a Person of Unsound Mind
sections.description: When an act, which would otherwise be a certain offence, is not that offence, by reason of the youth, the want of maturity of understanding, the unsoundness of mind or the intoxication of the person doing that act, every person has the same right of private defence against that act which he would have if the act were that offence.

sections.title: Acts Against Which There Is No Right of Private Defence
sections.description: There is no right of private defence against an act which does not reasonably cause the apprehension of death or of grievous hurt, if done by a public servant acting in good faith under colour of his office.

sections.title: When the Right of Private Defence of the Body Extends to Causing Death
sections.description: The right of private defence of the body extends to the voluntary causing of death or of any other harm to the assailant in circumstances including assault causing reasonable apprehension of death, grievous hurt, rape, kidnapping, wrongful confinement or acid attack.
sections.max_punishment: N/A — general exception

sections.title: When Such Right Extends to Causing Any Harm Other Than Death
sections.description: If the offence be not of any of the descriptions enumerated in section 100, the right of private defence of the body does not extend to the voluntary causing of death to the assailant, but does extend, under the restrictions mentioned in section 99, to the voluntary causing to the assailant of any harm other than death.

sections.title: Commencement and Continuance of the Right of Private Defence of the Body
sections.description: The right of private defence of the body commences as soon as a reasonable apprehension of danger to the body arises from an attempt or threat to commit the offence though the offence may not have been committed.

sections.title: When the Right of Private Defence of Property Extends to Causing Death
sections.description: The right of private defence of property extends to causing death if the offence is robbery, house-breaking by night, mischief by fire to buildings, or house-trespass creating fear of death or grievous hurt.

sections.title: When Such Right Extends to Causing Any Harm Other Than Death
sections.description: If the offence, the committing of which, or the attempting to commit which, occasions the exercise of the right of private defence, be theft, mischief, or criminal trespass, not of any of the descriptions enumerated in section 103, that right does not extend to the voluntary causing of death, but does extend, under the restrictions mentioned in section 99, to the voluntary causing to the wrong-doer of any harm other than death.

sections.title: Commencement and Continuance of the Right of Private Defence of Property
sections.description: The right of private defence of property commences when a reasonable apprehension of danger to the property commences and it continues while such reasonable apprehension of danger to the property continues.

sections.title: Right of Private Defence Against Deadly Assault When There Is Risk of Harm to Innocent Person
sections.description: If in the exercise of the right of private defence against an assault which reasonably causes the apprehension of death, the defender be so situated that he cannot effectually exercise that right without risk of harm to an innocent person, his right of private defence extends to the running of that risk.

sections.title: Abetment of a Thing
sections.description: A person abets the doing of a thing who instigates any person to do it; or engages with one or more others in a conspiracy for doing it; or intentionally aids, by any act or illegal omission, the doing of that thing.

sections.title: Abettor
sections.description: A person abets an offence who abets either the commission of an offence, or the commission of an act which would be an offence, if committed by a person capable by law of committing an offence.

sections.title: Abetment in India of Offences Outside India
sections.description: A person abets an offence within the meaning of this Code who in India abets the commission of any act without and beyond India which would constitute an offence if committed in India.

sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Act Abetted Is Committed in Consequence and Where No Provision Is Made
sections.description: Whoever abets any offence shall, if the act abetted is committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted

sections.title: Punishment of Abetment if Person Abetted Does Act with Different Intention from That of Abettor
sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence shall, if the person abetted does the act with a different intention from that of the abettor, be punished with the punishment provided for the offence which would have been committed if the act had been done with the intention of the abettor.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence abetted with abettor's intention

sections.title: Liability of Abettor When One Act Abetted and Different Act Done
sections.description: When an act is abetted and a different act is done, the abettor is liable for the act done, in the same manner and to the same extent as if he had directly abetted it, provided he knew the different act to be likely.

sections.title: Abettor When Liable to Cumulative Punishment for Act Abetted and for Act Done
sections.description: If the act for which the abettor is liable under section 111 is committed in addition to the act abetted, and constitutes a distinct offence, the abettor is liable to punishment for each of the offences.

sections.title: Liability of Abettor for an Effect Caused by Act Abetted Different from That Intended
sections.description: When an act is abetted with the intention of causing a particular effect, and an act for which the abettor is liable causes a different effect, the abettor is liable for the effect caused, in the same manner and to the same extent as if he had abetted the act with the intention of causing that effect.

sections.title: Abettor Present When Offence Is Committed
sections.description: Whenever any person who would be liable as an abettor is present when the act or offence for which he would be punishable is committed, he shall be deemed to have committed such act or offence.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for the principal offender

sections.title: Abetment of Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life — If Offence Not Committed
sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, shall, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment, and no express provision is made for the punishment of such abetment, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; 14 years and fine if hurt caused

sub_sections.description: If any act for which the abettor is liable in consequence of the abetment, and which causes hurt to any person, is done, the abettor shall be liable to imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to fourteen years, and shall also be liable to fine.

sections.title: Abetment of Offence Punishable with Imprisonment — If Offence Not Committed
sections.description: Whoever abets an offence punishable with imprisonment shall, if that offence be not committed in consequence of the abetment, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one-fourth part of the longest term provided for that offence, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Up to one-fourth the longest term for the offence or fine or both

sections.title: Abetting Commission of Offence by the Public or by More Than Ten Persons
sections.description: Whoever abets the commission of an offence by the public generally or by any number of persons exceeding ten, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Concealing Design to Commit Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life
sections.description: Whoever intending to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby facilitate the commission of an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, voluntarily conceals the existence of a design to commit such offence.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; 10 years if committed by public servant

sections.title: Public Servant Concealing Design to Commit Offence Which It Is His Duty to Prevent
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, intending to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby facilitate the commission of an offence which it is his duty as such public servant to prevent, voluntarily conceals the existence of a design to commit such offence.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to half the longest term for the offence, or fine or both

sections.title: Concealing Design to Commit Offence Punishable with Imprisonment
sections.description: Whoever intending to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby facilitate the commission of an offence punishable with imprisonment, voluntarily conceals the existence of a design to commit such offence.
sections.max_punishment: Up to one-fourth the longest term for the offence, or fine or both

sections.title: Definition of Criminal Conspiracy
sections.description: When two or more persons agree to do, or cause to be done, an illegal act, or an act which is not illegal by illegal means, such an agreement is designated a criminal conspiracy.

sections.title: Punishment of Criminal Conspiracy
sections.description: Whoever is a party to a criminal conspiracy to commit an offence punishable with death, imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment for two years or upwards shall be punished in the same manner as if he had abetted such offence.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for abetment of the offence; imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both for other conspiracies

sections.title: Waging War Against the Government of India
sections.description: Whoever wages war against the Government of India, or attempts to wage such war, or abets the waging of such war, shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

sections.title: Conspiracy to Commit Offences Punishable by Section 121
sections.description: Whoever within or without India conspires to commit any of the offences punishable by section 121, or conspires to overawe, by means of criminal force or the show of criminal force, the Central or any State Government.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Collecting Arms with Intention of Waging War Against Government of India
sections.description: Whoever collects men, arms or ammunition or otherwise prepares to wage war with the intention of waging or being prepared to wage war against the Government of India.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Concealing with Intent to Facilitate Design to Wage War
sections.description: Whoever by any act or illegal omission conceals the existence of a design to wage war against the Government of India, intending by such concealment to facilitate or knowing it to be likely that it will facilitate the waging of such war.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Assaulting President or Governor with Intent to Compel or Restrain Exercise of Lawful Power
sections.description: Whoever, with the intention of inducing or compelling the President of India, or Governor of any State, to exercise or refrain from exercising any lawful powers, assaults or wrongfully restrains, or overawes by means of criminal force.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Sedition
sections.description: Whoever by words, either spoken or written, or by signs, or by visible representation, or otherwise, brings or attempts to bring into hatred or contempt, or excites or attempts to excite disaffection towards the Government established by law in India.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life and fine, or up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Waging War Against Asiatic Power in Alliance with Government of India
sections.description: Whoever wages war against the Government of any Asiatic Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India, or attempts to wage such war, or abets the waging of such war.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life and fine, or up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Committing Depredation on Territories of Power at Peace with Government of India
sections.description: Whoever commits depredation, or makes preparations to commit depredation, on the territories of any Power in alliance or at peace with the Government of India.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sub_sections.description: Whoever makes preparation to commit depredation on territories of a Power at peace with the Government of India shall be punished with imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property used or intended to be used.

sections.title: Receiving Property Taken by War or Depredation
sections.description: Whoever receives any property knowing the same to have been taken in the commission of any of the offences mentioned in sections 125 and 126.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine and forfeiture of property

sections.title: Public Servant Voluntarily Allowing Prisoner of State or War to Escape
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, and having the custody of any State prisoner or prisoner of war, voluntarily allows such prisoner to escape from any place of confinement.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Public Servant Negligently Suffering Such Prisoner to Escape
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant and having the custody of any State prisoner or prisoner of war, negligently suffers such prisoner to escape from any place of confinement.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Aiding Escape of, Rescuing or Harbouring Such Prisoner
sections.description: Whoever knowingly aids or assists any State prisoner or prisoner of war in escaping from lawful custody, or rescues or attempts to rescue any such prisoner.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Abetting Mutiny, or Attempting to Seduce a Soldier, Sailor or Airman from His Duty
sections.description: Whoever abets the committing of mutiny by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman, or attempts to seduce any such person from his allegiance or his duty.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Abetment of Mutiny if Mutiny Is Committed in Consequence
sections.description: Whoever abets the committing of mutiny and mutiny is committed in consequence of that abetment.
sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

sections.title: Abetment of Assault by Soldier on His Superior Officer When in Execution of His Office
sections.description: Whoever abets an assault by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman on any superior officer being in the execution of his office.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Abetment of Such Assault if the Assault Is Committed
sections.description: Whoever abets such assault and such assault is committed in consequence thereof.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Abetment of Desertion of Soldier, Sailor or Airman
sections.description: Whoever abets the desertion of any officer, soldier, sailor or airman in the Army, Navy or Air Force of India.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Harbouring Deserter
sections.description: Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an officer, soldier, sailor or airman has deserted, harbours such person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Deserter Concealed on Board Merchant Vessel Through Negligence of Master
sections.description: The master or person in charge of a merchant vessel on board of which any deserter from the Army, Navy or Air Force is concealed, shall though ignorant of such concealment, be liable to a penalty.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 500

sections.title: Abetment of Act of Insubordination by Soldier, Sailor or Airman
sections.description: Whoever abets what would constitute an act of insubordination if committed by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman in the Army, Navy or Air Force of India.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

sections.title: Application of Foregoing Sections to the Indian Marine Service
sections.description: The provisions of the foregoing sections of this Chapter apply to persons subject to the Indian Marine Service Act.

sections.title: Persons Subject to Certain Acts
sections.description: Nothing in the foregoing sections of this Chapter is to be construed to apply to any act done by a person subject to the Army Act, the Naval Discipline Act or the Air Force Act.

sections.title: Wearing Garb or Carrying Token Used by Soldier, Sailor or Airman
sections.description: Whoever, not being a soldier, sailor or airman in the Military, Naval or Air Service of the Government of India, wears any garb or carries any token resembling any garb or token used by such a soldier, sailor or airman with the intention that it may be believed that he is such a soldier, sailor or airman.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Unlawful Assembly
sections.description: An assembly of five or more persons is designated an 'unlawful assembly' if the common object is to overawe Government, resist execution of legal process, commit mischief, criminal trespass, or other offences.

sections.title: Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
sections.description: Whoever, being aware of facts which render any assembly an unlawful assembly, intentionally joins that assembly, or continues in it, is said to be a member of an unlawful assembly.

sections.title: Punishment for Being Member of Unlawful Assembly
sections.description: Whoever is a member of an unlawful assembly shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Joining Unlawful Assembly Armed with Deadly Weapon
sections.description: Whoever, being armed with any deadly weapon, or anything likely to cause death, is a member of an unlawful assembly.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Joining or Continuing in Unlawful Assembly Knowing It Has Been Commanded to Disperse
sections.description: Whoever joins or continues in an unlawful assembly knowing that such assembly has been lawfully commanded to disperse.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Rioting
sections.description: Whenever force or violence is used by an unlawful assembly, or by any member thereof, in prosecution of the common object of such assembly, every member of such assembly is guilty of rioting.

sections.title: Punishment for Rioting
sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: 2 years imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Rioting Armed with Deadly Weapon
sections.description: Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything likely to cause death.
sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Every Member of Unlawful Assembly Guilty of Offence Committed in Prosecution of Common Object
sections.description: If an offence is committed by any member of an unlawful assembly in prosecution of the common object of that assembly, every person who is a member of the same assembly is guilty of that offence.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for the offence committed

sections.title: Hiring, or Conniving at Hiring, of Persons to Join Unlawful Assembly
sections.description: Whoever hires or engages or employs or promotes or connives at the hiring, engagement or employment of any person to join or become a member of any unlawful assembly.
sections.max_punishment: Same as member of that assembly

sections.title: Knowingly Joining or Continuing in Assembly of Five or More Persons After It Has Been Commanded to Disperse
sections.description: Whoever knowingly joins or continues in any assembly of five or more persons likely to cause a disturbance of the public peace, after such assembly has been lawfully commanded to disperse.
sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Assaulting or Obstructing Public Servant When Suppressing Riot
sections.description: Whoever assaults or threatens to assault, or obstructs or attempts to obstruct, any public servant in the discharge of his duty in endeavouring to disperse an unlawful assembly or to suppress a riot.
sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Wantonly Giving Provocation with Intent to Cause Riot
sections.description: Whoever malignantly or wantonly, by doing anything illegal, gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause the offence of rioting to be committed.
sections.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both; 3 years if riot actually occurs

sections.title: Promoting Enmity Between Different Groups on Ground of Religion, Race, etc.
sections.description: Whoever by words, either spoken or written, or by signs or by visible representations, promotes or attempts to promote disharmony or feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes or communities.
sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both; 5 years if committed at place of worship

sections.title: Punishment for Knowingly Carrying Arms in Any Procession or Mass Drill with Arms
sections.description: Whoever knowingly carries arms in any procession or organises or takes part in any mass drill or mass training with arms in contravention of any public notice or order prohibiting it.
sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine up to Rs. 2000 or both

sections.title: Imputations, Assertions Prejudicial to National Integration
sections.description: Whoever makes or publishes any imputation that any class of persons cannot, by reason of their being members of any religious, racial, language or regional group or caste or community, bear true faith and allegiance to the Constitution of India.
sections.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both; 5 years if at place of worship

sections.title: Owner or Occupier of Land on Which an Unlawful Assembly Is Held
sections.description: Whenever any unlawful assembly or riot takes place, the owner or occupier of the land upon which such unlawful assembly is held, who knew that such offence was being or likely to be committed and did not give the earliest notice thereof to the principal officer of the nearest police station.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 1000

sections.title: Liability of Person for Whose Benefit Riot Is Committed
sections.description: Whenever a riot is committed for the benefit or on behalf of any person who is the owner or occupier of land respecting which such riot takes place, such person shall be punishable.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 1000

sections.title: Liability of Agent of Owner or Occupier for Whose Benefit Riot Is Committed
sections.description: Whenever a riot is committed for the benefit or on behalf of any person who is the owner or occupier of any land, the agent or manager of such person shall be punishable.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 1000

sections.title: Harbouring Persons Hired for an Unlawful Assembly
sections.description: Whoever harbours, receives or assembles in any house or premises in his occupation or charge any persons knowing that such persons have been hired or are about to be hired to join or become members of an unlawful assembly.
sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Being Hired to Take Part in an Unlawful Assembly or Riot
sections.description: Whoever is engaged, or hired, or offers or attempts to be hired or engaged, to do or assist in doing any of the acts specified in section 141.
sections.max_punishment: 6 months imprisonment or fine or both; 2 years if hired to carry deadly weapon

sections.title: Affray
sections.description: When two or more persons, by fighting in a public place, disturb the public peace, they are said to 'commit an affray'.

sections.title: Punishment for Committing Affray
sections.description: Whoever commits an affray shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one hundred rupees, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: 1 month imprisonment or fine up to Rs. 100 or both

sections.title: Public Servant Taking Gratification Other Than Legal Remuneration in Respect of an Official Act [Repealed]
sections.description: Repealed by the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.

sections.title: Taking Gratification in Order, by Corrupt or Illegal Means, to Influence Public Servant [Repealed]
sections.description: Repealed by the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.

sections.title: Taking Gratification for Exercise of Personal Influence with Public Servant [Repealed]
sections.description: Repealed by the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.

sections.title: Punishment for Abetment by Public Servant of Offences Defined in Section 162 or 163 [Repealed]
sections.description: Repealed by the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.

sections.title: Public Servant Obtaining Valuable Thing, Without Consideration, from Person Concerned in Proceeding or Business Transacted by Such Public Servant [Repealed]
sections.description: Repealed by the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.

sections.title: Punishment for Abetment of Offences Defined in Section 161 or 165 [Repealed]
sections.description: Repealed by the Prevention of Corruption Act, 1988.

sections.title: Public Servant Disobeying Law with Intent to Cause Injury to Any Person
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law as to the way in which he is to conduct himself as such public servant, intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, injury to any person.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Public Servant Disobeying Direction Under Law
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law which prohibits him from requiring the attendance at any place of any person for investigation; or fails to record information regarding cognizable offences under sections 326A, 326B, 354, 354B, 370, 370A, 376, 376A to 376E or 509.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 6 months and up to 2 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Non-Treatment of Victim
sections.description: Whoever, being in charge of a hospital, whether Government or private, fails to immediately provide first-aid or medical treatment to the victims of an offence covered under sections 326A, 376, 376A to 376E.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Public Servant Framing an Incorrect Document with Intent to Cause Injury
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, charged with the preparation or translation of any document, frames or translates that document in a manner which he knows or believes to be incorrect, intending to cause injury to any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Public Servant Unlawfully Engaging in Trade
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, and being legally bound not to engage in trade, engages in trade.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Public Servant Unlawfully Buying or Bidding for Property
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, and being legally bound not to purchase or bid for certain property, purchases or bids for that property, either in his own name or in the name of another.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; property may be confiscated

sections.title: Personating a Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever pretends to hold any particular office as a public servant, knowing that he does not hold such office, and in such assumed character does or attempts to do any act under colour of such office.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Wearing Garb or Carrying Token Used by Public Servant with Fraudulent Intent
sections.description: Whoever, not belonging to a class of public servants, wears any garb or carries any token resembling garb or token used by that class, with the intention that it may be believed that he belongs to that class.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 200 or both

sections.title: Candidate and Electoral Right Defined
sections.description: 'Candidate' means a person who has been nominated as a candidate at any election. 'Electoral right' means the right of a person to stand, or not to stand as, or to withdraw from being, a candidate, or to vote or refrain from voting at an election.

sections.title: Bribery
sections.description: Whoever gives a gratification to any person with the object of inducing him or any other person to exercise any electoral right, or accepts a gratification as a reward for having exercised any such right.
sections.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Undue Influence at Elections
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily interferes or attempts to interfere with the free exercise of any electoral right commits the offence of undue influence at an election.
sections.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Personation at Elections
sections.description: Whoever at an election applies for a voting paper or votes in the name of any other person, whether living or dead, or in a fictitious name, or who having voted once applies at the same election for a voting paper in his own or any other name.
sections.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Bribery
sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of bribery shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both; provided that bribery by treating shall be punished with fine only.
sections.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Undue Influence or Personation at an Election
sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of undue influence or personation at an election shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both

sections.title: False Statement in Connection with an Election
sections.description: Whoever with intent to affect the result of an election makes or publishes any statement purporting to be a statement of fact which is false and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, in relation to the personal character or conduct of any candidate.
sections.max_punishment: Fine only

sections.title: Illegal Payments in Connection with an Election
sections.description: Whoever without the authority of a candidate incurs or authorises expenses on account of the holding of any public meeting, or upon any advertisement, circular or publication for the purpose of promoting or procuring the election of such candidate.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 500

sections.title: Failure to Keep Election Accounts
sections.description: Whoever being required by any law or rule having the force of law to keep accounts of expenses incurred at or in connection with an election fails to keep such accounts.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 500

sections.title: Absconding to Avoid Service of Summons
sections.description: Whoever absconds in order to avoid being served with a summons, notice or order proceeding from any public servant legally competent to issue such summons, notice or order.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if to avoid court attendance

sections.title: Preventing Service of Summons or Other Proceeding
sections.description: Whoever in any manner intentionally prevents the serving on himself, or on any other person, of any summons, notice or order proceeding from any public servant legally competent to issue such summons, notice or order.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if court related

sections.title: Non-Attendance in Obedience to an Order from Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound to attend in person or by an agent at a certain place and time in obedience to a summons, notice, order, or proclamation proceeding from any public servant legally competent to issue the same, intentionally omits to attend at that place or time.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if court-related

sections.title: Non-Appearance in Response to a Proclamation Under Section 82 of CrPC
sections.description: Whoever fails to appear at the specified place and the specified time as required by a proclamation published under sub-section (1) of section 82 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both; 7 years if the offence is punishable with 10 years or more

sections.title: Omission to Produce Document to Public Servant by Person Legally Bound
sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound to produce or deliver up any document to any public servant, intentionally omits to produce or deliver up the same.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if court-related

sections.title: Omission to Give Notice or Information to Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound to give any notice or to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, intentionally omits to give such notice or to furnish such information in the manner and at the time required by law.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if court-related

sections.title: Furnishing False Information
sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, furnishes, as true, information on the subject which he knows or has reason to believe to be false.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both; 2 years or fine if to prevent commission of offence

sections.title: Refusing Oath or Affirmation When Duly Required by Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever refuses to bind himself by an oath or affirmation to state the truth, when required so to bind himself by a public servant legally competent to require that he shall so bind himself.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Refusing to Answer Public Servant Authorised to Question
sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound to state the truth on any subject to any public servant, refuses to answer any question demanded of him touching that subject by such public servant in the exercise of the legal powers of such public servant.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Refusing to Sign Statement
sections.description: Whoever refuses to sign any statement made by him, when required to sign that statement by a public servant legally competent to require that he shall sign that statement.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: False Statement on Oath to Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or affirmation to state the truth on any subject to any public servant or other person authorized to administer such oath or affirmation, makes, touching that subject, any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: False Information with Intent to Cause Public Servant to Use Lawful Power to Injury of Another
sections.description: Whoever gives to any public servant any information which he knows or believes to be false, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, such public servant to use the lawful power of such public servant to the injury or annoyance of any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Resistance to the Taking of Property by Lawful Authority of a Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever offers any resistance to the taking of any property by the lawful authority of any public servant, knowing or having reason to believe that he is such public servant.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Obstructing Sale of Property Offered for Sale by Authority of Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever intentionally obstructs any sale of property offered for sale by the authority of any public servant as such.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Illegal Purchase or Bid for Property Offered for Sale by Authority of Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever, at any sale of property held by the authority of a public servant as such, purchases or bids for any property on account of any person whom he knows to be under a legal incapacity to purchase that property at that sale.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both

sections.title: Obstructing Public Servant in Discharge of Public Functions
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Omission to Assist Public Servant When Bound by Law to Give Assistance
sections.description: Whoever, being bound by law to render or furnish assistance to any public servant in the execution of his public duty, intentionally omits to give such assistance.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both; 6 months or Rs. 500 if omission regarding prevention of offence

sections.title: Disobedience to Order Duly Promulgated by Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever, knowing that, by an order promulgated by a public servant lawfully empowered to promulgate such order, he is directed to abstain from a certain act or to take certain order with certain property in his possession or under his management, disobeys such direction.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both; 6 months or Rs. 1000 if danger to human life

sections.title: Threat of Injury to Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant, or to any person in whom that public servant is interested, for the purpose of inducing that public servant to do any act, or to forbear or delay to do any act, connected with the exercise of the public functions of such public servant.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sub_sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any public servant or to any person in whom such public servant is interested, with intent to induce that public servant to do any act connected with the exercise of his public functions and such act is of a criminal nature, shall be punished with imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both.

sections.title: Threat of Injury to Induce Person to Refrain from Applying for Protection
sections.description: Whoever holds out any threat of injury to any person for the purpose of inducing that person to refrain or desist from making a legal application for protection against any injury to any public servant legally empowered as such to give such protection.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Giving False Evidence
sections.description: Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or by an express provision of law to state the truth, or being bound by law to make a declaration upon any subject, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, is said to give false evidence.

sub_sections.description: A statement is within the meaning of this section whether it is made verbally or otherwise, whether it is made in a judicial proceeding or in any other proceeding before any authority lawfully empowered to receive evidence or before any other person required or authorised by law to take such declaration.

sections.title: Fabricating False Evidence
sections.description: Whoever causes any circumstance to exist or makes any false entry in any book or record, or electronic record, or makes any document or electronic record containing a false statement, intending that such circumstance, false entry or false statement may appear in evidence in a judicial proceeding, or in a proceeding taken by law before a public officer or an arbitrator.

sections.title: Punishment for False Evidence
sections.description: Whoever intentionally gives false evidence in any stage of a judicial proceeding, or fabricates false evidence for the purpose of being used in any stage of a judicial proceeding, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: 7 years imprisonment and fine in judicial proceedings; 3 years and fine in other cases

sections.title: Giving or Fabricating False Evidence with Intent to Procure Conviction of Capital Offence
sections.description: Whoever gives or fabricates false evidence, intending thereby to cause any person to be convicted of an offence which is capital by the law for the time being in force in India.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; death if an innocent person is convicted and executed

sections.title: Giving or Fabricating False Evidence with Intent to Procure Conviction for Serious Offence
sections.description: Whoever gives or fabricates false evidence intending thereby to cause any person to be convicted of an offence punishable with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment for a term of seven years or upwards.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Threatening Any Person to Give False Evidence
sections.description: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause that person to give false evidence.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years or fine or both; same as false evidence if innocent person convicted

sections.title: Using Evidence Known to Be False
sections.description: Whoever corruptly uses or attempts to use as true or genuine evidence any evidence which he knows to be false or fabricated.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for giving false evidence (IPC 193)

sections.title: Issuing or Signing False Certificate
sections.description: Whoever issues or signs any certificate required by law to be given or signed, or relating to any fact of which such certificate is by law admissible in evidence, knowing or believing that such certificate is false in any material point.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for giving false evidence (IPC 193)

sections.title: Using as True a Certificate Known to Be False
sections.description: Whoever corruptly uses or attempts to use any such certificate as a true certificate, knowing the same to be false in any material point.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for giving false evidence (IPC 193)

sections.title: False Statement Made in Declaration Which Is by Law Receivable as Evidence
sections.description: Whoever, in any declaration made or subscribed by him, which declaration any Court of Justice, or any public servant, is competent to receive as evidence of any fact, makes any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, touching any point material to the object for which the declaration is made or used.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for giving false evidence (IPC 193)

sections.title: Using as True Such Declaration Knowing It to Be False
sections.description: Whoever corruptly uses or attempts to use as true any such declaration, knowing the same to be false in any material point.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for giving false evidence (IPC 193)

sections.title: Causing Disappearance of Evidence of Offence, or Giving False Information to Screen Offender
sections.description: Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, causes any evidence of the commission of that offence to disappear, with the intention of screening the offender from legal punishment, or with that intention gives any information respecting the offence which he knows or believes to be false.
sections.max_punishment: Up to 7 years and fine if offence punishable with death; proportional otherwise

sections.title: Intentional Omission to Give Information of Offence by Person Bound to Inform
sections.description: Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, intentionally omits to give any information respecting that offence which he is legally bound to give.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

sections.title: Giving False Information Respecting an Offence Committed
sections.description: Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that an offence has been committed, gives any information respecting that offence which he knows or believes to be false.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Destruction of Document to Prevent Its Production as Evidence
sections.description: Whoever secretes or destroys any document or electronic record which he may be lawfully compelled to produce as evidence in a Court of Justice, or in any proceeding lawfully held before a public officer or an arbitrator.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: False Personation for Purpose of Act or Proceeding in Suit or Prosecution
sections.description: Whoever falsely personates another, and in such assumed character makes any admission or statement, or confesses judgment, or causes any process to be issued or becomes bail or security, or does any other act in any suit or criminal prosecution.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Fraudulent Removal or Concealment of Property to Prevent Its Seizure
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently removes, conceals, transfers or delivers to any person any property or any interest therein, intending thereby to prevent that property or interest therein from being taken as a forfeiture or in satisfaction of a fine, under a sentence which has been pronounced, or which he knows to be likely to be pronounced.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Fraudulent Claim to Property to Prevent Its Seizure
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently accepts, receives or claims any property or any interest therein, knowing that he has no right or rightful claim to such property or interest, or practises any deception touching any right to any property, intending thereby to prevent that property or interest therein from being taken as a forfeiture or in satisfaction of a fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Fraudulently Suffering Decree for Sum Not Due
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently causes or suffers a decree or order to be passed against him at the suit of any person for a sum not due or for a larger sum than is due to such person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Dishonestly Making False Claim in Court
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly makes a false claim in any Court of Justice.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine

sections.title: Fraudulently Obtaining Decree for Sum Not Due
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently obtains a decree or order against any person for a sum not due, or for a larger sum than is due, or for any property or interest in property to which he is not entitled.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: False Charge of Offence Made with Intent to Injure
sections.description: Whoever, with intent to cause injury to any person, institutes or causes to be instituted any criminal proceeding against that person, or falsely charges any person with having committed an offence, knowing that there is no just or lawful ground for such proceeding or charge against that person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years and fine if charge relates to capital offence

sections.title: Harbouring Offender
sections.description: Whenever an offence has been committed, whoever harbours or conceals a person whom he knows or has reason to believe to be the offender, with the intention of screening him from legal punishment.
sections.max_punishment: Proportional to severity of the offence; up to imprisonment for life if offence punishable with death

sections.title: Taking Gift to Screen an Offender from Punishment
sections.description: Whoever accepts or attempts to obtain, or agrees to accept, any gratification for himself or any other person, in consideration of his concealing an offence or of his screening any person from legal punishment for any offence, or of his not proceeding against any person for the purpose of bringing him to legal punishment.
sections.max_punishment: Proportional to severity of offence screened

sections.title: Offering Gift or Restoration of Property in Consideration of Screening Offender
sections.description: Whoever gives or causes or offers or agrees to give any gratification to any person, or restores or causes the restoration of any property to any person, in consideration of that person's concealing an offence, or of his screening any person from legal punishment.
sections.max_punishment: Same as section 213

sections.title: Taking Gift to Help Recover Stolen Property
sections.description: Whoever takes or agrees or consents to take any gratification under pretence or on account of helping any person to recover any movable property of which he shall have been deprived by any offence punishable under this Code.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Harbouring Offender Who Has Escaped from Custody
sections.description: Whenever any person convicted of or charged with an offence, being in lawful custody for that offence, escapes from such custody, or whenever a public servant orders a certain person to be apprehended for an offence; whoever, knowing of such escape or order for apprehension, harbours or conceals that person with the intention of preventing him from being apprehended.
sections.max_punishment: Proportional to severity of original offence

sections.title: Penalty for Harbouring Robbers or Dacoits
sections.description: Whoever, knowing or having reason to believe that any persons are about to commit or have recently committed robbery or dacoity, harbours them or any of them, with the intention of facilitating the commission of such robbery or dacoity or of screening them from punishment.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Definition of Harbour in Sections 212, 216 and 216A
sections.description: For the purposes of sections 212, 216 and 216A, 'harbour' does not include the case of a person who, unaware of the offence committed, gives refuge to any person.

sections.title: Public Servant Disobeying Direction of Law with Intent to Save Person from Punishment
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law as to the way in which he is to conduct himself as such public servant, intending thereby to save, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby save, any person from legal punishment.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Public Servant Framing Incorrect Record with Intent to Save Person from Punishment
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, charged with the preparation of any record or other writing, frames that record or writing in a manner which he knows to be incorrect, with intent to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, loss or injury to the public or to any person, or to save any person from legal punishment.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Public Servant in Judicial Proceeding Corruptly Making Report Contrary to Law
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, corruptly or maliciously makes or pronounces in any stage of a judicial proceeding any report, order, verdict, or decision which he knows to be contrary to law.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years or fine or both

sections.title: Commitment for Trial or Confinement by Person Having Authority Who Knows He Is Acting Contrary to Law
