sections.description: Whoever, being in a position which legally authorises him to commit persons for trial or to confinement, corruptly or maliciously commits any person for trial or to confinement, knowing that in so doing he is acting contrary to law.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years or fine or both

sections.title: Intentional Omission to Apprehend on the Part of Public Servant Bound to Apprehend
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, legally bound to apprehend or to keep in confinement any person, intentionally omits to apprehend such person, or intentionally suffers such person to escape, or intentionally aids such person in escaping or attempting to escape from such confinement.
sections.max_punishment: Proportional based on severity of offence of person escaped

sections.title: Intentional Omission to Apprehend Person Under Sentence or Lawfully Committed
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, legally bound to apprehend or to keep in confinement any person under sentence of a Court of Justice for any offence or lawfully committed to custody, intentionally omits to apprehend such person or intentionally suffers such person to escape.
sections.max_punishment: Proportional based on severity of original offence

sections.title: Escape from Confinement or Custody Negligently Suffered by Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant, legally bound to keep in confinement any person charged with or convicted of any offence or lawfully committed to custody, negligently suffers such person to escape from confinement.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Resistance or Obstruction by a Person to His Lawful Apprehension
sections.description: Whoever intentionally offers any resistance or illegal obstruction to the lawful apprehension of himself for any offence with which he is charged or of which he has been convicted, or escapes or attempts to escape from any custody in which he is lawfully detained for any such offence.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Resistance or Obstruction to Lawful Apprehension of Another Person
sections.description: Whoever intentionally offers any resistance or illegal obstruction to the lawful apprehension of any other person for an offence, or rescues or attempts to rescue any other person from any custody in which that person is lawfully detained for an offence.
sections.max_punishment: Proportional based on severity of offence of the person

sections.title: Omission to Apprehend, or Sufferance of Escape, on Part of Public Servant in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
sections.description: Whoever, being a public servant legally bound to apprehend, or to keep in confinement, any person in any case not provided for in sections 221, 222 or 223, voluntarily omits to apprehend such person or suffers him to escape from confinement.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both; simple imprisonment up to 2 years if negligent

sections.title: Resistance or Obstruction to Lawful Apprehension, or Rescue in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
sections.description: Whoever, in any case not provided for in sections 224 or 225, intentionally offers any resistance or illegal obstruction to the lawful apprehension of himself or of any other person, or escapes or attempts to escape from any custody in which he is lawfully detained.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

sections.title: Unlawful Return from Transportation
sections.description: Whoever, having been lawfully transported to any place out of India, returns to India without lawful authority before the expiration of the term for which he was sentenced to be transported.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or lesser term for which offender was sentenced and fine

sections.title: Violation of Condition of Remission of Punishment
sections.description: Whoever, having accepted a conditional remission of punishment, knowingly violates any condition on which such remission was granted.
sections.max_punishment: Undergo unexpired portion of the original sentence

sections.title: Intentional Insult or Interruption to Public Servant Sitting in Judicial Proceeding
sections.description: Whoever intentionally offers any insult, or causes any interruption to any public servant, while such public servant is sitting in any stage of a judicial proceeding.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Disclosure of Identity of the Victim of Certain Offences
sections.description: Whoever prints or publishes the name or any matter which may make known the identity of any person against whom an offence under sections 376, 376A to 376E is alleged or found to have been committed.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine

sections.title: Personation of a Juror or Assessor
sections.description: Whoever, by personation or otherwise, shall intentionally cause, or shall knowingly suffer himself to be returned, empanelled or sworn as a juryman or assessor in any case in which he knows that he is not entitled by law to be so returned, empanelled or sworn.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Failure by Person Released on Bail or Bond to Appear in Court
sections.description: Whoever, having been released on bail or on bond by a Court, fails without sufficient cause to appear in Court in terms of the bail or bond.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Coin Defined
sections.description: Coin is metal used as money and stamped and issued by the authority of some State or Sovereign Power in order to be so used.

sections.title: Counterfeiting Coin
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits or knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting any coin.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Counterfeiting Indian Coin
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits, or knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting, any coin which is for the time being current in India.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Making or Selling Instrument for Counterfeiting Coin
sections.description: Whoever makes or mends, or performs any part of the process of making or mending, or buys, sells or disposes of, any die or instrument, for the purpose of being used for the purpose of counterfeiting any coin.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Making or Selling Instrument for Counterfeiting Indian Coin
sections.description: Whoever makes or mends, or performs any part of the process of making or mending, or buys, sells or disposes of, any die or instrument, for the purpose of being used for the purpose of counterfeiting any Indian coin.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Possession of Instrument or Material for Counterfeiting Coin
sections.description: Whoever is in possession of any instrument or material, for the purpose of using the same for counterfeiting any coin, or knowing or having reason to believe that the same is intended to be used for that purpose.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine; 10 years for Indian coin

sections.title: Abetting in India the Counterfeiting Out of India of Coin
sections.description: Whoever, being within India, abets the counterfeiting of coin out of India.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for counterfeiting

sections.title: Import or Export of Counterfeit Coin
sections.description: Whoever imports into India, or exports therefrom, any counterfeit coin, knowing or having reason to believe that the same is counterfeit.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine; 10 years for Indian coin

sections.title: Import or Export of Counterfeits of the Indian Coin
sections.description: Whoever imports into India, or exports therefrom, any counterfeit coin which is a counterfeit of the Indian coin.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Delivery of Coin Possessed with Knowledge That It Is Counterfeit
sections.description: Whoever, having knowledge that any coin is counterfeit, delivers such coin to any person, or attempts to induce any person to receive it.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine

sections.title: Delivery of Indian Coin Possessed with Knowledge That It Is Counterfeit
sections.description: Whoever, having knowledge that any coin which is for the time being current in India is counterfeit, delivers such coin to any person, or attempts to induce any person to receive it.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Delivery of Coin as Genuine Which, When First Possessed, the Deliverer Did Not Know to Be Counterfeit
sections.description: Whoever delivers to any other person as genuine, or attempts to induce any other person to receive as genuine, any counterfeit coin which, when he first became possessed of it, he did not know to be counterfeit.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine up to 10 times the value of the coin or both

sections.title: Possession of Counterfeit Coin by Person Who Knew It to Be Counterfeit When He Became Possessed Thereof
sections.description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent that fraud may be committed, is in possession of counterfeit coin, having known at the time when he became possessed thereof that such coin was counterfeit.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine

sections.title: Possession of Counterfeit Indian Coin by Person Who Knew It to Be Counterfeit
sections.description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent that fraud may be committed, is in possession of counterfeit coin which is current in India, having known at the time when he became possessed thereof that such coin was counterfeit.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Person Employed in Mint Causing Coin to Be of Different Weight or Composition from That Fixed by Law
sections.description: Whoever, being employed in any mint lawfully established in India, does any act, or omits what he is legally bound to do, with the intention of causing any coin issued from that mint to be of a different weight or composition from the weight or composition fixed by law.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Unlawfully Taking Coining Instrument from Mint
sections.description: Whoever, without lawful authority, takes out of any mint, lawfully established in India, any coining tool or instrument.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Fraudulently or Dishonestly Diminishing Weight or Altering Composition of Coin
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly performs on any coin any operation which diminishes the weight or alters the composition of that coin.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Fraudulently or Dishonestly Diminishing Weight or Altering Composition of Indian Coin
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly performs on any Indian coin any operation which diminishes the weight or alters the composition of that coin.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Altering Appearance of Coin with Intent That It Shall Pass as Coin of Different Description
sections.description: Whoever performs on any coin any operation which alters the appearance of that coin, with the intention that the said coin shall pass as a coin of a different description.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Altering Appearance of Indian Coin with Intent That It Shall Pass as Coin of Different Description
sections.description: Whoever performs on any Indian coin any operation which alters the appearance of that coin, with the intention that the said coin shall pass as a coin of a different description.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Delivery of Coin Possessed with Knowledge That It Is Altered
sections.description: Whoever, having knowledge that any coin has been altered, delivers such coin to any person, or attempts to induce any person to receive it.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine

sections.title: Delivery of Indian Coin Possessed with Knowledge That It Is Altered
sections.description: Whoever, having knowledge that any Indian coin has been altered, delivers such coin to any person, or attempts to induce any person to receive it.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Possession of Coin by Person Who Knew It to Be Altered When He Became Possessed Thereof
sections.description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent that fraud may be committed, is in possession of any altered coin, having known at the time when he became possessed thereof that such coin had been altered.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine

sections.title: Possession of Indian Coin by Person Who Knew It to Be Altered
sections.description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent that fraud may be committed, is in possession of any altered Indian coin, having known at the time when he became possessed thereof that such coin had been altered.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Delivery of Coin as Genuine Which, When First Possessed, the Deliverer Did Not Know to Be Altered
sections.description: Whoever delivers to any other person as genuine, or attempts to induce any other person to receive as genuine, any altered coin which, when he first became possessed of it, he did not know to be altered.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Counterfeiting Government Stamp
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits, or knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting, any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Having Possession of Instrument or Material for Counterfeiting Government Stamp
sections.description: Whoever has in his possession any instrument or material for the purpose of counterfeiting any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Making or Selling Instrument for Counterfeiting Government Stamp
sections.description: Whoever makes or performs any part of the process of making, or buys, sells or disposes of, any instrument for the purpose of being used for counterfeiting any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Sale of Counterfeit Government Stamp
sections.description: Whoever sells, or offers for sale, any stamp which he knows or has reason to believe to be a counterfeit of any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Having Possession of Counterfeit Government Stamp
sections.description: Whoever has in his possession any stamp which he knows to be a counterfeit of any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, intending to use, or dispose of the same as a genuine stamp.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Using as Genuine a Government Stamp Known to Be Counterfeit
sections.description: Whoever uses as genuine any stamp, knowing it to be a counterfeit of any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Effacing Writing from Substance Bearing Government Stamp, or Removing from Document a Stamp Used for It
sections.description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to Government, removes or effaces from any substance, bearing any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, any writing or document for which such stamp has been used, or removes from any writing or document a stamp which has been used for such writing or document.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Using Government Stamp Known to Have Been Before Used
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to Government, uses for any purpose a stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, which he knows to have been before used.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Erasure of Mark Denoting That Stamp Has Been Used
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to Government, erases or removes from a stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, any mark put or impressed upon such stamp for the purpose of denoting that the same has been used.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Prohibition of Fictitious Stamps
sections.description: Whoever makes, uses or sells, or has in his possession any fictitious stamp, or knowingly uses, buys or receives from any other person any packing or covering having thereon the impression of any fictitious stamp.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

sections.title: Fraudulent Use of False Instrument for Weighing
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently uses any instrument for weighing which he knows to be false.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Fraudulent Use of False Weight or Measure
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently uses any false weight or false measure of length or capacity, or fraudulently uses any weight or any measure of length or capacity as a different weight or measure from what it is.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Being in Possession of False Weight or Measure
sections.description: Whoever is in possession of any instrument for weighing, or of any weight, or of any measure of length or capacity, which he knows to be false, and intending that the same may be fraudulently used.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Making or Selling False Weight or Measure
sections.description: Whoever makes, sells or disposes of any instrument for weighing, or any weight, or any measure of length or capacity which he knows to be false, in order that the same may be used as true, or knowing that the same is likely to be used as true.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Public Nuisance
sections.description: A person is guilty of a public nuisance who does any act or is guilty of an illegal omission which causes any common injury, danger or annoyance to the public or to the people in general who dwell or occupy property in the vicinity, or which must necessarily cause injury, obstruction, danger or annoyance to persons who may have occasion to use any public right.

sections.title: Negligent Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
sections.description: Whoever unlawfully or negligently does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

sections.title: Malignant Act Likely to Spread Infection of Disease Dangerous to Life
sections.description: Whoever malignantly does any act which is, and which he knows or has reason to believe to be, likely to spread the infection of any disease dangerous to life.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Disobedience to Quarantine Rule
sections.description: Whoever knowingly disobeys any rule made and promulgated by the Government for putting any vessel into a state of quarantine, or for regulating the intercourse of vessels in a state of quarantine with the shore or with other vessels.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

sections.title: Adulteration of Food or Drink Intended for Sale
sections.description: Whoever adulterates any article of food or drink, so as to make such article noxious as food or drink, intending to sell such article or knowing it to be likely that the same will be sold as food or drink.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Sale of Noxious Food or Drink
sections.description: Whoever sells, or offers or exposes for sale, as food or drink, any article which has been rendered or has become noxious, or is in a state unfit for food or drink, knowing or having reason to believe that the same is noxious as food or drink.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Adulteration of Drugs
sections.description: Whoever adulterates any drug or medical preparation in such a manner as to lessen the efficacy or change the operation of such drug or medical preparation, or to make it noxious, intending that it shall be sold or used for, or knowing it to be likely that it will be sold or used for, any medicinal purpose.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Sale of Adulterated Drugs
sections.description: Whoever, knowing any drug or medical preparation to have been adulterated in such a manner as to lessen its efficacy, to change its operation, or to render it noxious, sells the same, or offers or exposes it for sale, or issues it from any dispensary for medicinal purposes as unadulterated.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Sale of Drug as a Different Drug or Preparation
sections.description: Whoever knowingly sells, or offers or exposes for sale, or issues from a dispensary for medicinal purposes, any drug or medical preparation, as a different drug or medical preparation.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Fouling Water of Public Spring or Reservoir
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily corrupts or fouls the water of any public spring or reservoir, so as to render it less fit for the purpose for which it is ordinarily used.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Making Atmosphere Noxious to Health
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily vitiates the atmosphere in any place so as to make it noxious to the health of persons in general dwelling or carrying on business in the neighbourhood or passing along a public way.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 500

sections.title: Rash Driving or Riding on a Public Way
sections.description: Whoever drives any vehicle, or rides, on any public way in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Rash Navigation of Vessel
sections.description: Whoever navigates any vessel in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Exhibition of False Light, Mark or Buoy
sections.description: Whoever exhibits any false light, mark or buoy, intending or knowing it to be likely that such exhibition will mislead any navigator.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years or fine or both

sections.title: Conveying Person by Water in Unsafe or Overloaded Vessel
sections.description: Whoever knowingly or negligently conveys, or causes to be conveyed for hire, any person by water in any vessel, when that vessel is in such a state or so loaded as to endanger the life of that person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Danger or Obstruction in Public Way or Line of Navigation
sections.description: Whoever, by doing any act, or by omitting to take order with any property in his possession or under his charge, causes danger, obstruction or injury to any person in any public way or public line of navigation.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Poisonous Substance
sections.description: Whoever does, with any poisonous substance, any act in a manner so rash or negligent as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Fire or Combustible Matter
sections.description: Whoever does, with fire or any combustible matter, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Explosive Substance
sections.description: Whoever does, with any explosive substance, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Machinery
sections.description: Whoever does, with any machinery, any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or to be likely to cause hurt or injury to any other person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Pulling Down or Repairing Buildings
sections.description: Whoever, in pulling down or repairing any building, knowingly or negligently omits to take such order with that building as is sufficient to guard against any probable danger to human life from the fall of that building, or of any part thereof.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Negligent Conduct with Respect to Animal
sections.description: Whoever knowingly or negligently omits to take such order with any animal in his possession as is sufficient to guard against any probable danger to human life, or any probable danger of grievous hurt from such animal.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Punishment for Public Nuisance in Cases Not Otherwise Provided For
sections.description: Whoever commits a public nuisance in any case not otherwise punishable by this Code.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 200

sections.title: Continuance of Nuisance After Injunction to Discontinue
sections.description: Whoever repeats or continues a public nuisance, having been enjoined by any public servant who has lawful authority to issue such injunction not to repeat or continue such nuisance.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both

sections.title: Sale of Obscene Books
sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, publicly exhibits or in any manner puts into circulation, or for purposes of sale, hire, distribution, public exhibition or circulation, makes, produces or has in his possession any obscene book, pamphlet, paper, drawing, painting, representation or figure or any other obscene object whatsoever.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 5 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction

sections.title: Sale of Obscene Objects to Young Person
sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, distributes, exhibits or circulates to any person under the age of twenty years any such obscene object as is referred to in the last preceding section.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine up to Rs. 2000; 7 years and Rs. 5000 for subsequent conviction

sections.title: Obscene Acts and Songs
sections.description: Whoever, to the annoyance of others, does any obscene act in any public place, or sings, recites or utters any obscene song, ballad or words, in or near any public place.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine or both

sections.title: Keeping Lottery Office
sections.description: Whoever keeps any office or place for the purpose of drawing any lottery not being a State lottery or a lottery authorised by the State Government.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine or both; fine only for publishing proposals for lotteries

sections.title: Injuring or Defiling Place of Worship with Intent to Insult the Religion of Any Class
sections.description: Whoever destroys, damages or defiles any place of worship, or any object held sacred by any class of persons with the intention of thereby insulting the religion of any class of persons or with the knowledge that any class of persons is likely to consider such destruction, damage or defilement as an insult to their religion.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Deliberate and Malicious Acts Intended to Outrage Religious Feelings
sections.description: Whoever, with deliberate and malicious intention of outraging the religious feelings of any class by insulting its religion or religious beliefs, commits any act or utters any word, sound or makes any gesture or places any object.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Disturbing Religious Assembly
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes disturbance to any assembly lawfully engaged in the performance of religious worship, or religious ceremonies.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sub_sections.description: This section applies equally to causing disturbance to funeral processions, burial grounds or any place set apart for performing funeral rites. Punishment is imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both.

sections.title: Trespassing on Burial Places
sections.description: Whoever, with the intention of wounding the feelings of any person, or of insulting the religion of any person, commits any trespass in any place of worship or on any place of sepulture or any place set apart for the performance of funeral rites or as a depository for the remains of the dead.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Uttering Words with Deliberate Intent to Wound Religious Feelings
sections.description: Whoever, with the deliberate intention of wounding the religious feelings of any person, utters any word or makes any sound in the hearing of that person or makes any gesture in the sight of that person or places any object in the sight of that person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Culpable Homicide
sections.description: Whoever causes death by doing an act with the intention of causing death, or with the intention of causing such bodily injury as is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that he is likely by such act to cause death, commits the offence of culpable homicide.

sections.title: Murder
sections.description: Culpable homicide is murder if the act by which the death is caused is done with the intention of causing death, or if it is done with the intention of causing such bodily injury as the offender knows to be likely to cause the death of the person to whom the harm is caused.

sections.title: Culpable Homicide by Causing Death of Person Other Than Person Whose Death Was Intended
sections.description: If a person, by doing anything which he intends or knows to be likely to cause death, commits culpable homicide by causing the death of any person, whose death he neither intends nor knows himself to be likely to cause, the culpable homicide committed by the offender is of the description of which it would have been if he had caused the death of the person whose death he intended or knew himself to be likely to cause.

sections.title: Punishment for Murder
sections.description: Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Murder by Life-Convict
sections.description: Whoever, being under sentence of imprisonment for life, commits murder, shall be punished with death.
sections.max_punishment: Death

sections.title: Punishment for Culpable Homicide Not Amounting to Murder
sections.description: Whoever commits culpable homicide not amounting to murder shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Causing Death by Negligence
sections.description: Whoever causes the death of any person by doing any rash or negligent act not amounting to culpable homicide, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Dowry Death
sections.description: Where the death of a woman is caused by any burns or bodily injury or occurs under abnormal circumstances within seven years of marriage and it is shown that soon before her death she was subjected to cruelty or harassment by her husband or any relative of her husband for, or in connection with, any demand for dowry.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment not less than 7 years but may extend to imprisonment for life

sections.title: Abetment of Suicide of Child or Insane Person
sections.description: If any person under eighteen years of age, any insane person, any delirious person, any idiot, or any person in a state of intoxication commits suicide, whoever abets the commission of such suicide.
sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Abetment of Suicide
sections.description: If any person commits suicide, whoever abets the commission of such suicide.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Attempt to Murder
sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge, and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of murder, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if hurt is caused

sections.title: Attempt to Commit Culpable Homicide
sections.description: Whoever does any act with such intention or knowledge and under such circumstances that, if he by that act caused death, he would be guilty of culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both; 7 years and fine if hurt is caused

sections.title: Attempt to Commit Suicide
sections.description: Whoever attempts to commit suicide and does any act towards the commission of such offence, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Thug
sections.description: Whoever, at any time after the passing of this Act, shall have been habitually associated with any other or others for the purpose of committing robbery or child-stealing by means of or accompanied with murder, is a thug.

sections.title: Punishment for Being a Thug
sections.description: Whoever is a thug shall be punished with imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life and fine

sections.title: Causing Miscarriage
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes a woman with child to miscarry, shall, if such miscarriage be not caused in good faith for the purpose of saving the life of the woman, be punished.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both; 7 years if woman is quick with child

sections.title: Causing Miscarriage Without Woman's Consent
sections.description: Whoever commits the offence defined in the last preceding section without the consent of the woman, whether the woman is quick with child or not.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Death Caused by Act Done with Intent to Cause Miscarriage
sections.description: Whoever, with intent to cause the miscarriage of a woman with child, does any act which causes the death of such woman.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if without woman's consent

sections.title: Act Done with Intent to Prevent Child Being Born Alive or to Cause It to Die After Birth
sections.description: Whoever, before the birth of any child, does any act with the intention of thereby preventing that child from being born alive or causing it to die after its birth, and does by such act prevent that child from being born alive, or causes it to die after its birth.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years or fine or both

sections.title: Causing Death of Quick Unborn Child by Act Amounting to Culpable Homicide
sections.description: Whoever does any act under such circumstances that if he thereby caused death he would be guilty of culpable homicide, and does by such act cause the death of a quick unborn child.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Exposure and Abandonment of Child Under Twelve Years by Parent
sections.description: Whoever, being the father or mother of a child under the age of twelve years, or having the care of such child, shall expose or leave such child in any place with the intention of wholly abandoning such child.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years or fine or both

sections.title: Concealment of Birth by Secret Disposal of Dead Body
sections.description: Whoever, by secretly burying or otherwise disposing of the dead body of a child whether such child die before or after or during its birth, intentionally conceals or endeavours to conceal the birth of such child.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Hurt
sections.description: Whoever causes bodily pain, disease or infirmity to any person is said to cause hurt.

sections.title: Grievous Hurt
sections.description: The following kinds of hurt only are designated as 'grievous': emasculation; permanent privation of sight or hearing; privation of any member or joint; destruction or permanent impairing of powers of any member or joint; permanent disfiguration of head or face; fracture or dislocation of bone or tooth; any hurt which endangers life or causes the sufferer severe bodily pain for twenty days.

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt
sections.description: Whoever does any act with the intention of thereby causing hurt to any person, or with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause hurt to any person, and does thereby cause hurt to any person, is said voluntarily to cause hurt.

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt, if the hurt which he intends to cause or knows himself to be likely to cause is grievous hurt, and if the hurt which he causes is grievous hurt, is said voluntarily to cause grievous hurt.

sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 334, voluntarily causes hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt
sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 335, voluntarily causes grievous hurt, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt by Dangerous Weapons or Means
sections.description: Whoever, except in the case provided for by section 335, voluntarily causes grievous hurt by means of any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting, or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, or by means of fire or any heated substance, or by means of any poison or any corrosive substance, or by means of any explosive substance.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt by Use of Acid
sections.description: Whoever causes permanent or partial damage or deformity to, or burns or maims or disfigures or disables, any part or parts of the body of a person or causes grievous hurt by throwing acid on or by administering acid to that person.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 10 years but may extend to life and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Throwing or Attempting to Throw Acid
sections.description: Whoever throws or attempts to throw acid on any person or attempts to administer acid to any person, or attempts to use any other means, with the intention of causing permanent or partial damage or deformity.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 5 years and up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt to Extort Property or to Constrain to an Illegal Act
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt, for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer, or from any person interested in the sufferer, any property or valuable security, or of constraining the sufferer or any person interested in such sufferer to do anything which is illegal.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Causing Hurt by Means of Poison with Intent to Commit an Offence
sections.description: Whoever administers to or causes to be taken by any person any poison or any stupefying, intoxicating or unwholesome drug, or other thing with intent to cause hurt to such person, or with intent to commit or to facilitate the commission of an offence.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt to Extort Property or to Constrain to an Illegal Act
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer or from any person interested in the sufferer any property or valuable security, or of constraining the sufferer or any person interested in such sufferer to do anything that is illegal.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sub_sections.description: Whoever commits this offence in order to commit or to facilitate the commission of an offence, using any instrument for shooting, stabbing or cutting or any instrument which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, shall be punished with imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine.

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt to Extort Confession or to Compel Restoration of Property
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer or from any person interested in the sufferer any confession or any information which may lead to the detection of an offence or misconduct, or for the purpose of constraining the sufferer or any person interested in the sufferer to restore or to cause the restoration of any property or valuable security.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt to Extort Confession or to Compel Restoration of Property
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt for the purpose of extorting from the sufferer or from any person interested in the sufferer any confession or any information which may lead to the detection of an offence or misconduct.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt to Deter Public Servant from His Duty
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt to any person being a public servant in the discharge of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person or any other public servant from discharging his duty as such public servant.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt to Deter Public Servant from His Duty
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt to any person being a public servant in the discharge of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter that person or any other public servant from discharging his duty.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt on Provocation
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes hurt on grave and sudden provocation, if he neither intends nor knows himself to be likely to cause hurt to any person other than the person who gave the provocation.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt on Provocation
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes grievous hurt on grave and sudden provocation, if he neither intends nor knows himself to be likely to cause grievous hurt to any person other than the person who gave the provocation.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 4 years or fine up to Rs. 2000 or both

sections.title: Act Endangering Life or Personal Safety of Others
sections.description: Whoever does any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life or the personal safety of others.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 250 or both

sections.title: Causing Hurt by Act Endangering Life or Personal Safety of Others
sections.description: Whoever causes hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or the personal safety of others.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 6 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Causing Grievous Hurt by Act Endangering Life or Personal Safety of Others
sections.description: Whoever causes grievous hurt to any person by doing any act so rashly or negligently as to endanger human life, or the personal safety of others.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Wrongful Restraint
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any person so as to prevent that person from proceeding in any direction in which that person has a right to proceed, is said wrongfully to restrain that person.

sections.title: Wrongful Confinement
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person in such a manner as to prevent that person from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits, is said wrongfully to confine that person.

sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Restraint
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully restrains any person shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Punishment for Wrongful Confinement
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Wrongful Confinement for Three or More Days
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person for three days or more.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Wrongful Confinement for Ten or More Days
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person for ten days or more.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Wrongful Confinement of Person for Whose Liberation Writ Has Been Issued
sections.description: Whoever keeps any person in wrongful confinement, knowing that a writ for the liberation of that person has been duly issued.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years in addition to any other punishment

sections.title: Wrongful Confinement in Secret
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person in such manner as to indicate an intention that the confinement of such person may not be known to any person interested in the person so confined.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years in addition to other punishment

sections.title: Wrongful Confinement to Extort Property or Constrain to Illegal Act
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person for the purpose of extorting from the person confined, or from any person interested in the person confined, any property or valuable security, or of constraining the person confined to do anything illegal.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Wrongful Confinement to Extort Confession or to Compel Restoration of Property
sections.description: Whoever wrongfully confines any person for the purpose of extorting from the person confined any confession or any information which may lead to the detection of an offence or misconduct.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Force
sections.description: A person is said to use force to another if he causes motion, change of motion, or cessation of motion to that other, or if he causes to any substance such motion, or change of motion, or cessation of motion as brings that substance into contact with any part of that other's body.

sections.title: Criminal Force
sections.description: Whoever intentionally uses force to any person, without that person's consent, in order to the committing of any offence, or intending by the use of such force to cause, or knowing it to be likely that by the use of such force he will cause injury, fear or annoyance to the person to whom the force is used, is said to use criminal force to that other.

sections.title: Assault
sections.description: Whoever makes any gesture, or any preparation intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that he who makes that gesture or preparation is about to use criminal force to that person, is said to commit an assault.

sections.title: Punishment for Assault or Criminal Force Otherwise Than on Grave Provocation
sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force to Deter Public Servant from Discharge of His Duty
sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person being a public servant in the execution of his duty, or with intent to prevent or deter that person from discharging his duty.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force on Woman with Intent to Outrage Her Modesty
sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any woman, intending to outrage or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby outrage her modesty.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 1 year and up to 5 years and fine

sections.title: Sexual Harassment and Punishment for Sexual Harassment
sections.description: A man committing physical contact and advances involving unwelcome and explicit sexual overtures; or a demand or request for sexual favours; or showing pornography against the will of a woman; or making sexually coloured remarks, shall be guilty of the offence of sexual harassment.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Assault or Use of Criminal Force to Woman with Intent to Disrobe
sections.description: Any man who assaults or uses criminal force to any woman or abets such act with the intention of disrobing or compelling her to be naked.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 3 years and up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Voyeurism
sections.description: Any man who watches, or captures the image of a woman engaging in a private act in circumstances where she would usually have the expectation of not being observed.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment not less than 1 year and up to 3 years for first conviction; 3 to 7 years for subsequent conviction

sections.title: Stalking
sections.description: Any man who follows a woman and contacts, or attempts to contact such woman to foster personal interaction repeatedly despite a clear indication of disinterest by such woman; or monitors the use by a woman of the internet, email or any other form of electronic communication.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years for first conviction; up to 5 years for subsequent conviction

sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force with Intent to Dishonour Person
sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, intending thereby to dishonour that person, otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force in Attempt to Commit Theft of Property Carried by a Person
sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, in attempting to commit theft on any property which that person is then wearing or carrying.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force in Attempt Wrongfully to Confine a Person
sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person, in attempting wrongfully to confine that person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: Assault or Criminal Force on Grave Provocation
sections.description: Whoever assaults or uses criminal force to any person on grave and sudden provocation given by that person.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 1 month or fine up to Rs. 200 or both

sections.title: Kidnapping
sections.description: Kidnapping is of two kinds: kidnapping from India, and kidnapping from lawful guardianship.

sections.title: Kidnapping from India
sections.description: Whoever conveys any person beyond the limits of India without the consent of that person, or of some person legally authorised to consent on behalf of that person, is said to kidnap that person from India.

sections.title: Kidnapping from Lawful Guardianship
sections.description: Whoever takes or entices any minor under sixteen years of age if a male, or under eighteen years of age if a female, or any person of unsound mind, out of the keeping of the lawful guardian of such minor or person of unsound mind, without the consent of such guardian.

sections.title: Abduction
sections.description: Whoever by force compels, or by any deceitful means induces, any person to go from any place, is said to abduct that person.

sections.title: Punishment for Kidnapping
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps any person from India or from lawful guardianship, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Kidnapping or Maiming a Minor for Purposes of Begging
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps any minor or, not being the lawful guardian of a minor, obtains the custody of the minor, in order that such minor may be employed or used for the purposes of begging.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; imprisonment for life if child is maimed

sections.title: Kidnapping or Abducting in Order to Murder
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person in order that such person may be murdered or may be so disposed of as to be put in danger of being murdered.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Kidnapping for Ransom
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person or keeps a person in detention after such kidnapping or abduction, and threatens to cause death or hurt to such person, or causes hurt or death to such person in order to compel the Government or any person to pay ransom.
sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life and fine

sections.title: Kidnapping or Abducting with Intent Secretly and Wrongfully to Confine Person
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person with intent to cause that person to be secretly and wrongfully confined.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Kidnapping, Abducting or Inducing Woman to Compel Her Marriage
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any woman with intent that she may be compelled to marry any person against her will, or in order that she may be forced or seduced to illicit intercourse.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Procuration of Minor Girl
sections.description: Whoever, by any means whatsoever, induces any minor girl under the age of eighteen years to go from any place or to do any act with intent that such girl may be, or knowing that it is likely that she will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Importation of Girl from Foreign Country
sections.description: Whoever imports into India from any country outside India any girl under the age of twenty-one years with intent that she may be, or knowing it to be likely that she will be, forced or seduced to illicit intercourse with another person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Kidnapping or Abducting in Order to Subject Person to Grievous Hurt, Slavery
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any person in order that such person may be subjected to grievous hurt, or slavery, or to the unnatural lust of any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Wrongfully Concealing or Keeping in Confinement Kidnapped or Abducted Person
sections.description: Whoever, knowing that any person has been kidnapped or has been abducted, wrongfully conceals or confines such person.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for kidnapping or abducting

sections.title: Kidnapping or Abducting Child Under Ten Years with Intent to Steal from Its Person
sections.description: Whoever kidnaps or abducts any child under the age of ten years with the intention of taking dishonestly any movable property from the person of such child.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Trafficking of Persons
sections.description: Whoever, for the purpose of exploitation, recruits, transports, harbours, transfers, or receives a person by using threats, force, coercion, abduction, fraud, deception, or abuse of power.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine; up to 10 years for more than one person

sections.title: Exploitation of a Trafficked Person
sections.description: Whoever knowingly engages or employs a person who has been trafficked.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 5 years and fine; 7 years and fine if victim is minor

sections.title: Habitual Dealing in Slaves
sections.description: Whoever habitually imports, exports, removes, buys, sells, traffics or deals in slaves.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Selling Minor for Purposes of Prostitution
sections.description: Whoever sells, lets to hire, or otherwise disposes of any minor under the age of eighteen years with intent that such minor shall be employed or used for the purpose of prostitution or illicit intercourse with any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Buying Minor for Purposes of Prostitution
sections.description: Whoever buys, hires or otherwise obtains possession of any minor under the age of eighteen years with intent that such minor shall be employed or used for the purpose of prostitution or illicit intercourse with any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Unlawful Compulsory Labour
sections.description: Whoever unlawfully compels any person to labour against the will of that person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Rape
sections.description: A man is said to commit 'rape' who has sexual intercourse with a woman under circumstances including against her will, without her consent, with consent obtained by fraud or fear, or when she is under 18 years of age.

sections.title: Punishment for Rape
sections.description: Whoever, except in the cases provided for in sub-section (2), commits rape, shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than seven years, but which may extend to imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 7 years up to life imprisonment and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Causing Death or Resulting in Persistent Vegetative State of Victim
sections.description: Whoever commits rape and in the course of such commission inflicts an injury which causes the death of the woman or causes the woman to be in a persistent vegetative state.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years up to life or death

sections.title: Punishment for Rape on Woman Under Twelve Years of Age
sections.description: Whoever commits rape on a woman under twelve years of age.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years up to life or death

sections.title: Sexual Intercourse by Husband upon His Wife During Separation
sections.description: Whoever has sexual intercourse with his own wife, who is living separately, whether under a decree of separation or otherwise, without her consent.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment not less than 2 years and up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Sexual Intercourse by Person in Authority
sections.description: Whoever, being in a position of authority or in a fiduciary relationship, or a public servant, takes advantage of his position and commits sexual intercourse with such woman.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 5 years and up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Gang Rape
sections.description: Where a woman is raped by one or more persons constituting a group or acting in furtherance of a common intention, each of those persons shall be deemed to have committed the offence of rape.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 20 years up to life and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Gang Rape on Woman Under Sixteen Years of Age
sections.description: Where a woman under sixteen years of age is raped by one or more persons constituting a group.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life (natural life) and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Gang Rape on Woman Under Twelve Years of Age
sections.description: Where a woman under twelve years of age is raped by one or more persons constituting a group.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life (natural life) or death and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Repeat Offenders
sections.description: Whoever has been previously convicted of an offence punishable under section 376 or section 376A or section 376D and is subsequently convicted of an offence punishable under any of the said sections.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life (natural life) or death

sections.title: Unnatural Offences
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily has carnal intercourse against the order of nature with any man, woman or animal.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Theft
sections.description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.

sections.title: Punishment for Theft
sections.description: Whoever commits theft shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Theft in Dwelling House
sections.description: Whoever commits theft in any building, tent or vessel, which building, tent or vessel is used as a human dwelling, or used for the custody of property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Theft by Clerk or Servant of Property in Possession of Master
sections.description: Whoever, being a clerk or servant, or being employed in the capacity of a clerk or servant, commits theft in respect of any property in the possession of his master or employer.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Theft After Preparation Made for Causing Death, Hurt or Restraint
sections.description: Whoever commits theft, having made preparation for causing death, or hurt, or restraint, or fear of death, or of hurt, or of restraint, to any person, in order to the committing of such theft, or in order to the effecting of his escape after the committing of such theft.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Extortion
sections.description: Whoever intentionally puts any person in fear of any injury to that person, or to any other, and thereby dishonestly induces the person so put in fear to deliver to any person any property or valuable security, or anything signed or sealed which may be converted into a valuable security, commits extortion.

sections.title: Punishment for Extortion
sections.description: Whoever commits extortion shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Putting Person in Fear of Injury in Order to Commit Extortion
sections.description: Whoever, in order to the committing of extortion, puts any person in fear, or attempts to put any person in fear, of any injury.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Extortion by Putting a Person in Fear of Death or Grievous Hurt
sections.description: Whoever commits extortion by putting any person in fear of death or of grievous hurt to that person or to any other.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Putting Person in Fear of Death or of Grievous Hurt in Order to Commit Extortion
sections.description: Whoever, in order to the committing of extortion, puts or attempts to put any person in fear of death or of grievous hurt to that person or to any other person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Extortion by Threat of Accusation of an Offence Punishable with Death or Imprisonment for Life
sections.description: Whoever commits extortion by putting any person in fear of an accusation of having committed an offence punishable with death, or with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Putting Person in Fear of Accusation of Offence in Order to Commit Extortion
sections.description: Whoever, in order to the committing of extortion, puts or attempts to put any person in fear of an accusation against that person or any other person of having committed an offence punishable with death or with imprisonment for life or for a term which may extend to ten years.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Robbery
sections.description: In all robbery there is either theft or extortion. Theft is 'robbery' if, in order to the committing of the theft, or in committing the theft, or in carrying away property obtained by the theft, the offender, for that end, voluntarily causes or attempts to cause to any person death or hurt or wrongful restraint.

sections.title: Dacoity
sections.description: When five or more persons conjointly commit or attempt to commit a robbery, or where the whole number of persons committing or attempting to commit a robbery, and persons present and aiding such commission, amount to five or more, every person so committing, attempting or aiding, is said to commit dacoity.

sections.title: Punishment for Robbery
sections.description: Whoever commits robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine; and, if the robbery be committed on the highway between sunset and sunrise, the imprisonment may be extended to fourteen years.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine; 14 years if on highway between sunset and sunrise

sections.title: Attempt to Commit Robbery
sections.description: Whoever attempts to commit robbery shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt in Committing Robbery
sections.description: If any person, in committing or in attempting to commit robbery, voluntarily causes hurt, such person, and any other person jointly concerned in committing or attempting to commit such robbery, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Dacoity
sections.description: Whoever commits dacoity shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Dacoity with Murder
sections.description: If any one of five or more persons who are conjointly committing dacoity, commits murder in so committing dacoity, every one of those persons shall be punished with death, or imprisonment for life, or rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Death or imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Robbery or Dacoity with Attempt to Cause Death or Grievous Hurt
sections.description: If, at the time of committing robbery or dacoity, the offender uses any deadly weapon, or causes grievous hurt to any person, or attempts to cause death or grievous hurt to any person, the imprisonment with which such offender shall be punished shall not be less than seven years.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 7 years

sections.title: Attempt to Commit Robbery or Dacoity When Armed with Deadly Weapon
sections.description: If, at the time of attempting to commit robbery or dacoity, the offender is armed with any deadly weapon, the imprisonment with which such offender shall be punished shall not be less than seven years.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment not less than 7 years

sections.title: Making Preparation to Commit Dacoity
sections.description: Whoever makes any preparation for committing dacoity shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Belonging to Gang of Dacoits
sections.description: Whoever, at any time after the passing of this Act, shall belong to any gang of persons associated for the purpose of habitually committing dacoity.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Belonging to Gang of Thieves
sections.description: Whoever, at any time after the passing of this Act, shall belong to any gang of persons associated for the purpose of habitually committing theft or robbery.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Assembling for Purpose of Committing Dacoity
sections.description: Whoever, at any time after the passing of this Act, shall be one of five or more persons assembled for the purpose of committing dacoity.
sections.max_punishment: Rigorous imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Dishonest Misappropriation of Property
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use any movable property, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Dishonest Misappropriation of Property Possessed by Deceased Person at the Time of His Death
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use property, knowing that such property was in the possession of a deceased person at the time of that person's decease, and has not since been in the possession of any person legally entitled to such possession.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust
sections.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property, or dishonestly uses or disposes of that property in violation of any direction of law prescribing the mode in which such trust is to be discharged.

sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Breach of Trust
sections.description: Whoever commits criminal breach of trust shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust by Carrier
sections.description: Whoever, being entrusted with property as a carrier, wharfinger or warehouse-keeper, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of such property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust by Clerk or Servant
sections.description: Whoever, being a clerk or servant or employed in the capacity of a clerk or servant, and being in any manner entrusted in such capacity with property or with any dominion over property, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of that property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Criminal Breach of Trust by Public Servant, or by Banker, Merchant or Agent
sections.description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property in his capacity of a public servant or in the way of his business as a banker, merchant, factor, broker, attorney or agent, commits criminal breach of trust in respect of that property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Stolen Property
sections.description: Property, the possession whereof has been transferred by theft, or by extortion, or by robbery, and property which has been criminally misappropriated or in respect of which criminal breach of trust has been committed, is designated as 'stolen property'.

sections.title: Dishonestly Receiving Stolen Property
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly receives or retains any stolen property, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be stolen property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Dishonestly Receiving Property Stolen in the Commission of a Dacoity
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly receives or retains any stolen property, the possession whereof he knows or has reason to believe to have been transferred by the commission of dacoity.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Habitually Dealing in Stolen Property
sections.description: Whoever habitually receives or deals in property which he knows or has reason to believe to be stolen property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Assisting in Concealment of Stolen Property
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily assists in concealing or disposing of or making away with property which he knows or has reason to believe to be stolen property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Cheating
sections.description: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to consent that any person shall retain any property, or intentionally induces the person so deceived to do or omit to do anything which he would not do or omit if he were not so deceived, and which act or omission causes or is likely to cause damage or harm to that person in body, mind, reputation or property, is said to cheat.

sections.title: Cheating by Personation
sections.description: A person is said to 'cheat by personation' if he cheats by pretending to be some other person, or by knowingly substituting one person for another.
