sections.title: Punishment for Cheating
sections.description: Whoever cheats shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Cheating with Knowledge That Wrongful Loss May Ensue to Person Whose Interest Offender Is Bound to Protect
sections.description: Whoever cheats with the knowledge that he is likely thereby to cause wrongful loss to a person whose interest he is bound, either by any law, or by any legal contract, to protect.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Cheating by Personation
sections.description: Whoever cheats by personation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Cheating and Dishonestly Inducing Delivery of Property
sections.description: Whoever cheats and thereby dishonestly induces the person deceived to deliver any property to any person, or to make, alter or destroy the whole or any part of a valuable security, or anything which is signed or sealed, and which is capable of being converted into a valuable security.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Dishonest or Fraudulent Removal or Concealment of Property to Prevent Distribution Among Creditors
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly or fraudulently removes, conceals or delivers to any person, or transfers or causes to be transferred to any person, without adequate consideration, any property, intending thereby to prevent the distribution of that property according to law among his creditors.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Dishonestly or Fraudulently Preventing Debt Being Available for Creditors
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly or fraudulently prevents any debt or demand due to himself or to any other person from being made available according to law for payment of his debts.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Dishonest or Fraudulent Execution of Deed of Transfer Containing False Statement of Consideration
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly or fraudulently signs, executes or becomes a party to any deed or instrument which purports to transfer or subject to any charge any property, or any interest therein, and which contains any false statement relating to the consideration for such transfer or charge.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Dishonest or Fraudulent Removal or Concealment of Property
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly or fraudulently conceals or removes any property of himself or any other person, or dishonestly or fraudulently assists in the concealment or removal thereof, or dishonestly releases any demand or claim to which he is entitled.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief
sections.description: Whoever with intent to cause, or knowing that he is likely to cause, wrongful loss or damage to the public or to any person, causes the destruction of any property, or any such change in any property or in the situation thereof as destroys or diminishes its value or utility, or affects it injuriously, commits mischief.

sections.title: Punishment for Mischief
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief Causing Damage to the Amount of Fifty Rupees
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief and thereby causes loss or damage to the amount of fifty rupees or upwards.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Killing or Maiming Animal of the Value of Ten Rupees
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by killing, poisoning, maiming or rendering useless any animal or animals of the value of ten rupees or upwards.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Killing or Maiming Cattle
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by killing, poisoning, maiming or rendering useless, any elephant, camel, horse, mule, buffalo, bull, cow or ox, whatever may be the value thereof, or any other animal of the value of fifty rupees or upwards.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Injury to Works of Irrigation or by Wrongfully Diverting Water
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by doing any act which causes, or which he knows to be likely to cause, a diminution of the supply of water for agricultural purposes, or for food or drink for human beings or for animals which are property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Injury to Public Road, Bridge, River or Channel
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by doing any act which renders or which he knows to be likely to render any public road, bridge, navigable river or navigable channel impassable or less safe for travelling or conveying property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Causing Inundation or Obstruction to Public Drainage
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by doing any act which causes or which he knows to be likely to cause an inundation or an obstruction to any public drainage attended with injury or damage.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Destroying, Moving or Rendering Less Useful a Lighthouse or Sea-Mark
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by destroying or moving any light-house or other light used as a sea-mark, or any sea-mark or buoy or other thing placed as a guide for navigators.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Destroying or Moving a Land-Mark Fixed by Public Authority
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by destroying or moving any land-mark fixed by the authority of a public servant, or by any act which renders such land-mark less useful as such.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Mischief by Fire or Explosive Substance with Intent to Cause Damage
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by fire or any explosive substance intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, damage to any property to the amount of one hundred rupees or upwards.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Mischief by Fire or Explosive Substance with Intent to Destroy House
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief by fire or any explosive substance, intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, the destruction of any building which is ordinarily used as a place of worship or as a human dwelling or as a place for the custody of property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Mischief with Intent to Destroy or Make Unsafe a Decked Vessel or One of Twenty Tons Burden
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief to any decked vessel or any vessel of a burden of twenty tons or upwards, intending to destroy or render unsafe, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby destroy or render unsafe, that vessel.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Mischief Described in Section 437 Committed by Fire or Explosive Substance
sections.description: Whoever commits, or attempts to commit, by fire or explosive substance, the mischief described in section 437.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Intentionally Running Vessel Aground or Ashore with Intent to Commit Theft
sections.description: Whoever intentionally runs any vessel aground or ashore, intending to commit theft of any property contained therein or to dishonestly misappropriate any such property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Mischief Committed After Preparation Made for Causing Death or Hurt
sections.description: Whoever commits mischief, having made preparation for causing to any person death, or hurt, or wrongful restraint, or fear of death, or of hurt, or of wrongful restraint.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine

sections.title: Criminal Trespass
sections.description: Whoever enters into or upon property in the possession of another with intent to commit an offence or to intimidate, insult or annoy any person in possession of such property, or having lawfully entered into or upon such property, unlawfully remains there with intent thereby to intimidate, insult or annoy any such person, or with intent to commit an offence.

sections.title: House-Trespass
sections.description: Whoever commits criminal trespass by entering into or remaining in any building, tent or vessel used as a human dwelling, or any building used as a place for worship, or as a place for the custody of property.

sections.title: Lurking House-Trespass
sections.description: Whoever commits house-trespass having taken precautions to conceal such house-trespass from some person who has a right to exclude or eject the trespasser from the building, tent or vessel which is the subject of the trespass.

sections.title: Lurking House-Trespass by Night
sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass after sunset and before sunrise.

sections.title: House-Breaking
sections.description: A person is said to commit house-breaking who commits house-trespass if he effects his entrance into the house or any part of it by using criminal force or committing an assault, or by any key other than one provided by the person in lawful possession, or by opening any lock by picking, or enters or quits through any passage not intended by any person who has a right to admit or exclude such person.

sections.title: House-Breaking by Night
sections.description: Whoever commits house-breaking after sunset and before sunrise is said to commit house-breaking by night.

sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Trespass
sections.description: Whoever commits criminal trespass shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to five hundred rupees, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine up to Rs. 500 or both

sections.title: Punishment for House-Trespass
sections.description: Whoever commits house-trespass shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine up to Rs. 1000 or both

sections.title: House-Trespass in Order to Commit Offence Punishable with Death
sections.description: Whoever commits house-trespass in order to the committing of any offence punishable with death.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: House-Trespass in Order to Commit Offence Punishable with Imprisonment for Life
sections.description: Whoever commits house-trespass in order to the committing of any offence punishable with imprisonment for life.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: House-Trespass in Order to Commit Offence Punishable with Imprisonment
sections.description: Whoever commits house-trespass in order to the committing of any offence punishable with imprisonment.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine; 7 years and fine if offence is theft or dishonest misappropriation

sections.title: House-Trespass After Preparation for Hurt, Assault or Wrongful Restraint
sections.description: Whoever commits house-trespass, having made preparation for causing hurt to any person or for assaulting any person, or for wrongfully restraining any person, or for putting any person in fear of hurt, or of assault, or of wrongful restraint.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking
sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass or house-breaking shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, and shall also be liable to fine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years and fine

sections.title: Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking in Order to Commit Offence Punishable with Imprisonment
sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass or house-breaking, in order to the committing of any offence punishable with imprisonment.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine; 10 years if theft

sections.title: Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking After Preparation for Hurt, Assault or Wrongful Restraint
sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass or house-breaking, having made preparation for causing hurt to any person or for assaulting any person, or for wrongfully restraining any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Punishment for Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking by Night
sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass by night, or house-breaking by night.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking by Night in Order to Commit Offence Punishable with Imprisonment
sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass by night, or house-breaking by night, in order to the committing of any offence punishable with imprisonment.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 5 years and fine; 14 years if theft

sections.title: Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking by Night After Preparation for Hurt, Assault or Wrongful Restraint
sections.description: Whoever commits lurking house-trespass by night, or house-breaking by night, having made preparation for causing hurt to any person or for assaulting any person, or for wrongfully restraining any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 14 years and fine

sections.title: Grievous Hurt Caused Whilst Committing Lurking House-Trespass or House-Breaking
sections.description: Whoever, whilst committing lurking house-trespass or house-breaking, causes grievous hurt to any person or attempts to cause death or grievous hurt to any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: All Persons Jointly Concerned in Lurking House-Trespass by Night Punishable Where Death or Grievous Hurt Caused by One of Them
sections.description: If, at the time of the committing of lurking house-trespass by night or house-breaking by night, any person guilty of such offence shall voluntarily cause or attempt to cause death or grievous hurt to any person, every person jointly concerned in committing such offence shall be punished.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Dishonestly Breaking Open Receptacle Containing Property
sections.description: Whoever dishonestly, or with intent to commit mischief, breaks open or unfastens any closed receptacle which contains or which he believes to contain property.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Same Offence When Committed by Person Entrusted with Custody
sections.description: Whoever, being entrusted with any closed receptacle which contains or which he believes to contain property, without having authority to open the same, dishonestly, or with intent to commit mischief, breaks open or unfastens that receptacle.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Forgery
sections.description: Whoever makes any false document or false electronic record or part of a document or electronic record, with intent to cause damage or injury to the public or to any person, or to support any claim or title, or to cause any person to part with property, or to enter into any express or implied contract, or with intent to commit fraud or that fraud may be committed, commits forgery.

sections.title: Making a False Document
sections.description: A person is said to make a false document or false electronic record who dishonestly or fraudulently makes, signs, seals or executes a document or electronic record or part thereof.

sections.title: Punishment for Forgery
sections.description: Whoever commits forgery shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Forgery of Record of Court or of Public Register
sections.description: Whoever forges a document or an electronic record, purporting to be a record or proceeding of or in a Court of Justice, or a register of birth, baptism, marriage or burial, or a register kept by a public servant as such.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Forgery of Valuable Security, Will
sections.description: Whoever forges a document purporting to be a valuable security or a will, or an authority to adopt a son, or which purports to give authority to any person to make or transfer any valuable security.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Forgery for Purpose of Cheating
sections.description: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document or electronic record forged shall be used for the purpose of cheating.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Forgery for Purpose of Harming Reputation
sections.description: Whoever commits forgery, intending that the document or electronic record forged shall harm the reputation of any party, or knowing that it is likely to be used for that purpose.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Forged Document
sections.description: A false document or electronic record made wholly or in part by forgery is designated a forged document or forged electronic record.

sections.title: Using as Genuine a Forged Document or Electronic Record
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly uses as genuine any document or electronic record which he knows or has reason to believe to be a forged document or electronic record.
sections.max_punishment: Same as for forgery of such document

sections.title: Making or Possessing Counterfeit Seal with Intent to Commit Forgery Punishable Under Section 467
sections.description: Whoever makes or counterfeits any seal, plate or other instrument for making an impression, intending that the same shall be used for the purpose of committing any forgery which would be punishable under section 467.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Making or Possessing Counterfeit Seal with Intent to Commit Forgery Punishable Otherwise
sections.description: Whoever makes or counterfeits any seal, plate or other instrument for making an impression, intending that the same shall be used for the purpose of committing any forgery which would be punishable under any section other than section 467.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Having Possession of Document Described in Section 466 or 467, Knowing It to Be Forged and Intending to Use It as Genuine
sections.description: Whoever has in his possession any document or electronic record, knowing the same to be forged and intending that the same shall fraudulently or dishonestly be used as genuine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or 7 years for documents covered by 467; 7 years for others

sections.title: Counterfeiting Device or Mark Used for Authenticating Documents Described in Section 467
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits upon, or in the substance of, any material, any device or mark used for the purpose of authenticating any document described in section 467.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Counterfeiting Device or Mark Used for Authenticating Documents Other Than Those Described in Section 467
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits upon, or in the substance of, any material, any device or mark used for the purpose of authenticating any document or electronic record other than those described in section 467.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Fraudulent Cancellation, Destruction, etc., of Will, Authority to Adopt, or Valuable Security
sections.description: Whoever fraudulently or dishonestly, or with intent to cause damage or injury to the public or to any person, cancels, destroys or defaces, or attempts to cancel, destroy or deface, or secretes or attempts to secrete any document which is or purports to be a will or an authority to adopt a son, or any valuable security.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Falsification of Accounts
sections.description: Whoever, being a clerk, officer or servant, or employed or acting in the capacity of a clerk, officer or servant, wilfully, and with intent to defraud, destroys, alters, mutilates or falsifies any book, electronic record, paper, writing, valuable security or account which belongs to or is in the possession of his employer.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Trade Mark
sections.description: A mark used for denoting that goods are the manufacture or merchandise of a particular person is called a trade mark.

sections.title: Property Mark
sections.description: A mark used for denoting that movable property belongs to a particular person is called a property mark.

sections.title: Using a False Trade Mark
sections.description: Whoever uses any false trade mark shall, unless he proves that he acted without intent to defraud, be punished.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Using a False Property Mark
sections.description: Whoever uses any false property mark shall, unless he proves that he acted without intent to defraud, be punished.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Using a False Trade Mark or Property Mark
sections.description: Whoever uses any false trade mark or false property mark shall, unless he proves that he acted without intent to defraud, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Counterfeiting a Trade Mark or Property Mark Used by Another
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits any trade mark or property mark used by any other person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Counterfeiting a Mark Used by a Public Servant
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits any mark used by a public servant in his official capacity.
sections.max_punishment: Treated as counterfeiting the coin of the Government of India

sections.title: Making or Possession of Any Instrument for Counterfeiting a Trade Mark or Property Mark
sections.description: Whoever makes or has in his possession any die, plate or other instrument for the purpose of counterfeiting a trade mark or property mark.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Selling Goods Marked with a Counterfeit Trade Mark
sections.description: Whoever sells, or exposes, or has in possession for sale, any goods or things with a counterfeit trade mark affixed to or impressed upon the same.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Making a False Mark upon Any Receptacle Containing Goods
sections.description: Whoever makes any false mark upon any case, package or other receptacle containing goods, in a manner reasonably calculated to cause any public servant or any other person to believe that such receptacle contains goods which it does not contain.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Making Use of Any Such False Mark
sections.description: Whoever makes use of any such false mark as mentioned in section 487.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Tampering with Property Mark with Intent to Cause Injury
sections.description: Whoever removes, destroys, defaces or adds to any property mark, intending or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause injury to any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Counterfeiting Currency Notes or Bank Notes
sections.description: Whoever counterfeits, or knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting, any currency-note or bank-note.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Using as Genuine, Forged or Counterfeit Currency Notes or Bank Notes
sections.description: Whoever sells to, or buys or receives from, any other person, or otherwise traffics in or uses as genuine, any forged or counterfeit currency-note or bank-note, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be forged or counterfeit.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Possession of Forged or Counterfeit Currency Notes or Bank Notes
sections.description: Whoever has in his possession any forged or counterfeit currency-note or bank-note, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be forged or counterfeit and intending to use the same as genuine.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years or fine or both

sections.title: Making or Possessing Instruments or Materials for Forging or Counterfeiting Currency Notes or Bank Notes
sections.description: Whoever makes, or performs any part of the process of making, or buys, or sells, or disposes of, or has in his possession, any machinery, instrument or material for the purpose of being used for forging or counterfeiting any currency-note or bank-note.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment for life or rigorous imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Making or Using Documents Resembling Currency Notes or Bank Notes
sections.description: Whoever makes, or causes to be made, or uses for any purpose, or delivers to any person, any document purporting to be, or in any way resembling, or so nearly resembling as to be calculated to deceive, any currency-note or bank-note.
sections.max_punishment: Fine up to Rs. 100; if intent to deceive imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Breach of Contract to Attend on and Supply Wants of Helpless Person
sections.description: Whoever, being under a legal obligation to provide for the support and necessities of any infant or child under the age of twelve years, or of any person by reason of unsoundness of mind or bodily weakness is incapable of providing for his own support and necessities, fraudulently or dishonestly fails to provide such support and necessities.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine or both

sections.title: Breach of Contract to Attend on and Supply Wants of Helpless Person (Servant/Workman)
sections.description: Whoever, being bound by a lawful contract to attend on or to supply the wants of any person who, by reason of youth, or of unsoundness of mind, or of a disease or bodily weakness, is unable to provide for his own safety or the supply of his own wants, voluntarily omits so to do.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine or both

sections.title: Breach by Servant or Agent of Trust Reposed in Him
sections.description: Whoever, being bound by a lawful contract as a servant, workman or agent, to keep any goods entrusted to him or to furnish any accounting of any such goods, dishonestly or fraudulently omits to do so.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 months or fine or both

sections.title: Cohabitation Caused by a Man Deceitfully Inducing a Belief of Lawful Marriage
sections.description: Every man who by deceit causes any woman who is not lawfully married to him to believe that she is lawfully married to him and to cohabit or have sexual intercourse with him in that belief.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Marrying Again During Lifetime of Husband or Wife
sections.description: Whoever, having a husband or wife living, marries in any case in which such marriage is void by reason of its taking place during the life of such husband or wife.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Same Offence with Concealment of Former Marriage from Person with Whom Subsequent Marriage Is Contracted
sections.description: Whoever commits the offence defined in the last preceding section having concealed from the person with whom the subsequent marriage is contracted, the fact of the former marriage.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 10 years and fine

sections.title: Marriage Ceremony Fraudulently Gone Through Without Lawful Marriage
sections.description: Whoever, dishonestly or with a fraudulent intention, goes through the ceremony of being married, knowing that he is not thereby lawfully married.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 7 years and fine

sections.title: Adultery [Struck Down by Supreme Court]
sections.description: This section has been struck down as unconstitutional by the Supreme Court of India in Joseph Shine v. Union of India (2018). It previously dealt with adultery as an offence.
sections.max_punishment: Section struck down by Supreme Court in 2018

sections.title: Enticing or Taking Away or Detaining with Criminal Intent a Married Woman
sections.description: Whoever takes or entices away any woman who is and whom he knows or has reason to believe to be the wife of any other man, from that man, or from any person having the care of her on behalf of that man, with intent that she may have illicit intercourse with any person.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Husband or Relative of Husband of a Woman Subjecting Her to Cruelty
sections.description: Whoever, being the husband or the relative of the husband of a woman, subjects such woman to cruelty. 'Cruelty' includes wilful conduct likely to drive the woman to commit suicide or cause grave injury; or harassment with a view to coercing her to meet any unlawful demand for dowry.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Defamation
sections.description: Whoever, by words either spoken or intended to be read, or by signs or by visible representations, makes or publishes any imputation concerning any person intending to harm, or knowing or having reason to believe that such imputation will harm, the reputation of such person, is said, except in the cases hereinafter excepted, to defame that person.

sections.title: Punishment for Defamation
sections.description: Whoever defames another shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Printing or Engraving Matter Known to Be Defamatory
sections.description: Whoever prints or engraves any matter, knowing or having good reason to believe that such matter is defamatory of any person.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Sale of Printed or Engraved Substance Containing Defamatory Matter
sections.description: Whoever sells or offers for sale any printed or engraved substance containing defamatory matter, knowing that it contains such matter.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Criminal Intimidation
sections.description: Whoever threatens another with any injury to his person, reputation or property, or to the person or reputation of any one in whom that person is interested, with intent to cause alarm to that person, or to cause that person to do any act which he is not legally bound to do, or to omit to do any act which that person is legally entitled to do, as the means of avoiding the execution of such threat, commits criminal intimidation.

sections.title: Intentional Insult with Intent to Provoke Breach of the Peace
sections.description: Whoever intentionally insults, and thereby gives provocation to any person, intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause him to break the public peace, or to commit any other offence.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both

sections.title: Statements Conducive to Public Mischief
sections.description: Whoever makes, publishes or circulates any statement, rumour or report with intent to cause, or which is likely to cause, any officer, soldier, sailor or airman in the Army, Navy or Air Force of India to mutiny or disregard his duty; or fear or alarm to the public, or to any section of the public whereby any person may be induced to commit an offence against the State or against the public tranquillity.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 3 years or fine or both

sections.title: Punishment for Criminal Intimidation
sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and if the threat be to cause death or grievous hurt, or to cause the destruction of any property by fire, or to cause an offence punishable with death or imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years, or to impute unchastity to a woman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine or both; 7 years or fine or both if threat is of death or grievous hurt etc.

sections.title: Criminal Intimidation by an Anonymous Communication
sections.description: Whoever commits the offence of criminal intimidation by an anonymous communication, or having taken precaution to conceal the name or abode of the person from whom the threat comes.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 2 years in addition to section 506 punishment

sections.title: Act Caused by Inducing Person to Believe That He Will Be Rendered an Object of Divine Displeasure
sections.description: Whoever voluntarily causes or attempts to cause any person to do anything which that person is not legally bound to do, by inducing or attempting to induce that person to believe that he or any person in whom he is interested will become an object of divine displeasure if he does not do the thing.
sections.max_punishment: Imprisonment up to 1 year or fine or both

sections.title: Word, Gesture or Act Intended to Insult the Modesty of a Woman
sections.description: Whoever, intending to insult the modesty of any woman, utters any word, makes any sound or gesture, or exhibits any object, intending that such word or sound shall be heard, or that such gesture or object shall be seen, by such woman, or intrudes upon the privacy of such woman.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 3 years and fine

sections.title: Misconduct in Public by a Drunken Person
sections.description: Whoever, in a state of intoxication, appears in any public place, or in any place which it is a trespass in him to enter, and there conducts himself in such a manner as to cause annoyance to any person.
sections.max_punishment: Simple imprisonment up to 24 hours or fine up to Rs. 10 or both

sections.title: Punishment for Attempting to Commit Offences Punishable with Imprisonment for Life or Other Imprisonment
sections.description: Whoever attempts to commit an offence punishable by this Code with imprisonment for life or imprisonment, or to cause such an offence to be committed, and in such attempt does any act towards the commission of the offence, shall, where no express provision is made by this Code for the punishment of such attempt, be punished with imprisonment of any description provided for the offence, for a term which may extend to one-half of the imprisonment for life or one-half of the longest term of imprisonment provided for that offence.
sections.max_punishment: Half the longest term of imprisonment or fine or both

special_acts_reference.description: Non-IPC special acts cited in candidate affidavits

acts.description: Punishment for contravening provisions regarding acquisition, possession, or carrying of arms or ammunition.
acts.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both; 7 years in aggravated cases

acts.description: Punishment for use of arms or ammunition in contravention of the Act.
Public servant taking gratification other than legal remuneration in respect of an official act.
acts.max_punishment: 6 months to 5 years imprisonment and fine

acts.description: Criminal misconduct by a public servant — possession of disproportionate assets.

acts.act: TNDA (Tamil Nadu District Authorities Act) — Section 3
acts.description: Detention of persons threatening public order or maintenance of supplies essential to the community.
Detention of goondas, bootleggers, drug offenders, etc., to prevent them from acting in a manner prejudicial to public order.

acts.act: Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act — Section 20
acts.description: Punishment for contravention in relation to cannabis plant and cannabis.
acts.max_punishment: 6 months to 20 years rigorous imprisonment and fine depending on quantity

acts.description: Punishment for contravention in relation to manufactured drugs and preparations.
acts.max_punishment: 1 to 20 years rigorous imprisonment and fine depending on quantity

acts.description: Promoting enmity between classes in connection with elections.
acts.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment or fine or both

acts.description: Removal of ballot papers from polling stations.
acts.max_punishment: 1 year imprisonment or fine or both

acts.description: Unlawful manufacture, possession, use, sale, transport or import of explosives.
Computer related offences — dishonestly or fraudulently doing any act.
acts.max_punishment: 3 years imprisonment and fine up to Rs.5 lakh

acts.description: Punishment for offences of atrocities committed against members of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes.
